body:
I now live in San Francisco. Please remove me from the mailing list. Thanks.

Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
________________________________
From: Michele Lanzara <michele.lanzara@convercio.com>
Sent: Wednesday, October 31, 2018 3:33:25 PM
To: Seattle Sales Enablement Society
Cc: Chuck Marcouiller; Juliana Stancampiano; Michele Lanzara (Convercio Inc)
Subject: UPDATE: Sales Enablement Society - Seattle Fall Chapter

Dear community,

Hope this email finds you well.

As many of you know, we will be holding our Fall chapter this coming November 8th at 5:30 PM in DocuSign's office in Seattle. Thank Tanya Casey and Sergio Larrain for the sponsorship.

This time Chuck, Juliana and I have put this amazing agenda together. We will have an amazing workshop with Dominique Levin to drive sales productivity. You don't want to miss this opportunity.

You should have an updated invitation in your inbox from the Seattle Sales Enablement Society email address. But, we encourage you to get enrolled in our platform for future updates. Just visit https://www.sesociety.org/home and click "Register" link at the top right of the main page. You want to be in the platform to receive the latest news from us and from other chapters. Even more, engage with your community around the world.

Below the details for Nov 8th venue.

Thanks

Chuck, Juliana, and Michele

____________________________________
IMPORTANT: LOCATION HAS CHANGED FROM COLUMBIA TOWER CLUB TO DOCUSIGN SEATTLE OFFICE
Thanks Tanya Casey and Sergio Larrain for sponsoring this venue.

Date: November 8
Time: 5:30pm-7:30pm
Location: 999 3rd Ave #1700, Seattle, WA 98104, USA
Food and drink: light food and coffee/Te will be provided.

Agenda suggested:

5:30 pm - 5:50 pm - Welcome - Chuck
5:50 pm - 7:00 pm - Sales as a Science Workshop - Dominique Levine

  *   Do you want to drive sales productivity and top-line revenue growth as a true business partner to the board and sales leadership team?
  *   You will learn a six-sigma inspired process to analyze sales productivity, diagnose the root-cause of bottlenecks, and create action plans that increase conversion rates, shorten sales cycles, and grow average deal size. You will do hands-on exercises analyzing specific company scenarios and will role-play presenting your analysis and action plan to management. The best presentations will be awarded a free copy of the brand-new Sales as a Science book.

  *   Who is Dominique Levin?
  *   She is a co-founder and managing partner at Winning by Design, a sales strategy consulting and coaching company trusted by over 250 B2B companies, including Adobe, Adroll, and Zenefits. She was previously CEO at LogLogic (sold to TIBCO), CMO at Totango and AgilOne amongst others. She is a co-author of Predictive Marketing, the SaaS Sales Method for CSMs and AMs, and Sales as a Science. Dominique holds an MBA from Harvard University (with Distinction) and an MSc in Industrial Design Engineering from Delft University (Cum Laude).

7:00 pm to 7:20 pm - (if time allows us) Event updates - Chuck

  *   Sales Enablement society conference (September)
  *   Sales Soiree @ Dreamforce (September)
  *   Sales 3.0 (October)

7:20 pm to 7:30 pm - Wrap Up: Next chapter, feedback, etc - Chuck

[photo]
Michele Lanzara
CEO & CoFounder at Convercio, Inc.
[https://dn3tzca2xtljm.cloudfront.net/social_icons/24px_transparent/linkedin.png]<http://linkedin.com/in/michelelanzara>                [https://dn3tzca2xtljm.cloudfront.net/social_icons/24px_transparent/twitter.png] <http://twitter.com/memasap>
Address  10400 NE 4th St, #500, Bellevue, WA 98004
Mobile  954 593 3831 <tel:954%20593%203831>
Email  michele.lanzara@convercio.com <mailto:michele.lanzara@convercio.com>
Website  www.convercio.com <http://www.convercio.com>
[https://s3.amazonaws.com/images.wisestamp.com/email-apps/meeting_button/meeting_button_1.png] <http://lanzara.link/onlinecall>         Click to schedule a meeting with me<http://lanzara.link/onlinecall>
subject: Re: UPDATE: Sales Enablement Society - Seattle Fall Chapter

body:
I am available all day Tuesday, Wednesday afternoon, and Thursday morning.

Also, to keep you up-to-date on other loops, I have 1st interviews scheduled with Oomnitza, Trellix, and Zscaler, and am in the process of  setting up 2nd interviews with WatchGuard, SentinelOne, and Alteryx.

Thanks.

---Steve

body:
Thanks Will…

I am curious about something. At MSFT, every interviewer had a specific objective assigned to them in an attempt to get a more well rounded view of the candidate.

Do y’all do the same thing or is each interview a stand alone interview just checking more for compatability?

Thanks.

---Steve

Sent from Mail<https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=550986> for Windows 10

From: William Robinson<mailto:Will.Robinson@watchguard.com>
Sent: Thursday, June 18, 2020 9:32 AM
To: Steve Winfield<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: RE: Next Steps?

Hi Steve!

I’m about to hop into another meeting just now, but afterwards I am going to start scheduling you for the Gauntlet.  I’ll have you speak with (roughly in this order):


  *   John Norton – Sales Engineer
  *   Jill Tillery – TCAM (the person you are replacing as she retires)
  *   Mark Romano – Senior Director of Channel & Field Marketing
  *   Sean Banahan – Manager of Channel Account Management
  *   Dave McNamara – VP of US Sales
  *   Erik Halvorsen – VP of Sales, Americas

There’s usually a break after that for the team to discuss, and then they’ll put you in with

  *   Sean Price – SVP of Sales, Global

In addition, we’ll have you complete a personality and references survey, as well.

Let me know if you have any questions, and keep your eyes out for a flood of calendar invites coming through today.

Best,
Will


From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, June 17, 2020 9:41 PM
To: William Robinson <Will.Robinson@watchguard.com>
Subject: Next Steps?

Hi Will…

I hope you had a good day today.

My call with Brian went well and he said he was going to put me through the gauntlet of interviews.

I have availability almost all day on Thursday (6/18) if you can find someone with an open time slot.


  *   Friday - Busy until 4pm PST.
  *   Monday – Any time after 12pm PST.
  *   Tuesday - After 10am PST.
  *   Wednesday- Free all day

Thanks.

---Steve

body:
So friendship with you is on your terms and on your timeline or you are not interested?

I am confused since you are not like this with Holly or anyone else.



Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
________________________________
From: Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, August 8, 2018 11:43 PM
To: Steve Winfield
Subject: Re: Friends

No thx. That is enough of an answer. My questions aren’t that complicated or time consuming. I ’m just going to go with expecting that the way things will go between us will be how they have been going between us lately. So my expectations will be really low and we will be that kind of level of friendship. And that’s no problem. And good to know. This helps in my efforts to move on. And I’m not mad, I swear. This is good to know, truly.

Hope your trip with your boss is going well

Thanks,
Elizabeth


On Aug 8, 2018, at 8:52 PM, Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:

I want to be friends and think we can be.

I am on a work trip work my boss this week and am exhausted.

Let's try to talk on the phone some on Friday when I am working from home.

Okay?

Get Outlook for iOS<https://nam01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Faka.ms%2Fo0ukef&data=02%7C01%7C%7C8f2254ddc4c247942d0508d5fdab8117%7C84df9e7fe9f640afb435aaaaaaaaaaaa%7C1%7C0%7C636693835392940952&sdata=Jw1qL%2BvPL4cUsBrRzMkbeHVAnF%2BmY8CEo%2BTHFX01Vog%3D&reserved=0>
________________________________
From: Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com<mailto:elidunc@hotmail.com>>
Sent: Wednesday, August 8, 2018 10:03:48 AM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Friends

So I was interested in being friends with you. But lately it appears that might not be possible, I’m feeling.
I feel that we can’t discuss certain things without you getting upset and then you disappearing which I don’t like.That is my opinion/feelings and I hope you don’t try to argue points.
I feel compelled to tell you in the chance that you aren’t aware of this situation.
I don’t know where that leaves us. So maybe it will help if I ask a couple clarifying questions. Would you still like to try to be friends? If so, what topics should be off limits? How do you feel if I ignore you? Should we just expect ignoring each other intermittently? It would be great to set up some ground rules and expectations. Then we can both decide if it’s worth it.
Maybe we don’t talk about why we broke up. And maybe we don’t talk about dating others. And maybe on emails we don’t expect responses. Maybe it’s just occasionally we send each other things from the inter webs we think is funny.
Just some suggestions.

Thanks,
Elizabeth

---Steve

I remain very excited about the prospect of working for a company like WatchGuard and returning to the Great State of Texas.

Thanks.

---Steve

[cid:image001.jpg@01D47744.50031DD0]


  1.  Steve and I were co-workers at Microsoft on the Worldwide Education Team for 3 years, and then we spent 3 more years working on parallel teams.
  2.  Steve is great with interpersonal relationships and can basically get along with anybody.
  3.  Steve is motivated by both personal and team accomplishment and success.
  4.  His strongest attributes are his creativity, his teamwork, and his sense of humor (even though his jokes are REALLY dumb).
  5.  Yes. I whole heartedly recommend Steve for this role.
  6.  Steve will do great in this role. His enthusiasm, optimism, and team-mentality are infectious.

Wow. That seems really long. I would cut the last paragraph about the reunions completely.

I thought he was born in 1936…so he would be 83.


From: gwzoo@sbcglobal.net <gwzoo@sbcglobal.net>
Sent: Tuesday, October 29, 2019 8:45 AM
To: t-winfield@sbcglobal.net; 'Steve Winfield' <swinfield@hotmail.com>; annettewoodard122@gmail.com
Subject: Please proof Floyd's / Dad's obituary for me

Still have a couple of dates that I need to fill in and will look through his paperwork today to get those or change the obit.  Probably more to say in this so please add if you think appropriate.

Thanks,
Gene


Floyd E Winfield, Sr, (aka Froggie or Twin), age 82, lost his battle with cancer on Saturday, October 27, 2019 at Doctors Hospital at Renaissance located near his home in McAllen, Texas.

Mr. Winfield is survived by his three children: Floyd Jr (Gene), Terry, and Steve Winfield, his first wife, Suzie Winfield, and four grandchildren, Chad, Emma, Bryce, and Mia Winfield. Froggie also leaves behind this brother, Donald Winfield and his sisters, Betty Harrison, Nora Montgomery, Evelyn Vavrecka, and Annette Woodard (Right order by age?) along with a lengthy list of nephews, nieces, cousins and in-laws. He was predeceased by his wife Sadie Winfield and his brothers, Alfred, Ray and Lloyd (the other twin) Winfield.

Floyd graduated from Missouri City High School in 1955. Floyd was gifted with a mechanical ability and loved to figure out how something worked, and then how to repair it or make it better. Those skills served him well throughout his life in many ways. From working on cars in his youth, to radar systems in the USAF, then to working with the equipment in Hospital Labs, he never came across something he could not repair and probably improve. After graduating high school, he join the USAF in 1958. While stationed in Florida, Floyd met and married Suzie Markowitz in 1961 and together, they raised three sons. During his enlistment, Floyd served overseas for three years, including one year in the Vietnam Theatre. He retired with the rank of Staff Sergeant after almost 13 years of service to our county. After his Honorable Discharge, he eventually joined EI Dupont de Nemours & Co’s Instrument Products Division. Floyd worked his way through the ranks and eventually led the highly successful, nine-state Southwest Division of the Automatic Clinical Analyzer program and retired from Dupont in XXXX. It was while working for Dupont out of Corpus Christie that Floyd met and married Sadie ________ in 199X.

Retirement was not a word that Floyd really understood. He took it upon himself to fill his time helping his family and anyone else he got to know. His generosity was a huge part of his personality and he displayed it often when helping others. He loved pitching horseshoes, dancing, and darts and always had a joke or six that he was willing to share, whether you wanted to hear it or not! He and Sadie eventually sold their Corpus home and everything in it to purchase an RV so they could travel the country to see the sites and visit family and friends. When you talked to him, the question was never “How are you?” it was always “Where are you?”  When you would ask why he was where he was, he would tell you something like “there was traffic in Cincinnati so we turned left.”

He loved his family and always found his way back to Texas in time for the annual Winfield family reunion in Palacios. He loved to MC the family reunions where he made it fun for each of us.  Without a doubt, this is one of the many ways that we all remember him, standing on that stage and auctioning off someone’s craft TO help raise money for the next reunion. Many of the most sought-after items were pieces of art that he made himself. Everyone lucky enough to have won the bid for one of these pieces proudly displays them still today.

The family will hold a grave side service on Saturday, November 9, 2019, at 1:00 PM [tentative] at the Morton Cemetery in Richmond, Texas where he will be interred next to Sadie.
subject: RE: Please proof Floyd's / Dad's obituary for me

body:
The data comes from interactions. From a business perspective, anything gathered during a text exchange or email, knowing they just purchased a new stereo system, knowing that renewal is coming up. It is no different than how you learn about people during conversations, texts, or emails. Regarding regulations, we would have to verify, but I think we are okay because we are not collecting any PII. Knowing they have a vacation coming up or that their boss is mad at them don’t fall under regulations, but they do give us connections points to sore we are listening and we care.

My thought is to start this out using the online content creator community to prove the system. There are tens of millions of creators out there who already have audiences they try to stay connected with, but it is difficult. This concept could easily automate it in a multi-modal fashion and drive increased revenue with next to no effort of the creator via our automated routines, and we would take 20-30% of every $ that goes through our system. By using a micro-purchase concept with instant gratification, we can help creators unlock hundreds of thousands of previously untapped customers and could drastically improve revenue from those already engaged.

This allows us to prove the base concept and achieve profitability within the first 6 months since most of the costs will be consumption driven and paid for by the end customers of the content creators.

From here we would scale to the platforms themselves Twitch, Patreon, Only Fans, Telegram, etc., and offer a profit sharing model with them if they add our solution in to the benefits they give content creators and businesses.

We could then take the learnings from these friendly environments and start applying them in different industry use cases.

We have proven the concept. We have proven scale. And now we explode into the business world.

________________________________
From: Ross Brown <Ross@oberoncorp.com>
Sent: Monday, June 9, 2025 3:29:32 PM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: Differences

Ok, now I have questions  - what is the source data, how to you screen for privacy issues and how are you managing GDPR and PII data compliance?  Would seem to be directly impacted by those laws?

________________________________
From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Sent: Monday, June 9, 2025 2:21 PM
To: Ross Brown <Ross@oberoncorp.com>
Subject: Differences


Hi Ross…



Artisan is looking at a ingle busine function and creating a replacement for an employee. That I cool but very different (though we could easily get into their swim lane if we wanted).



My concept is adding a “human connection” into the AI relationship. With the Intelligent Forgetting system, AI is able to record and quickly access relevant information about the customers feelings, desire, and interests, not just about work…but about life. If in the course of an online chat the customer talks about being stressed out regarding next Tuesday’s meeting, AI could drop him a quick text the morning of the meeting wishing him good luck, or send a message the next day asking how it went.  If the customer mentions being a big Cowboy fan, a joke image emailed on Monday regarding something that happened during the game could make it easier to set that next ale call. For high dollar customers (or high-dollar potential customers) it could even be a phone call, “Hey…I only have a couple minutes, but I wanted to check in on yesterday’s meeting as I remember you were a bit worried about it.…” Being able to add a personal touch to take it beyond to a transactional exchange to an actual connection and interaction is a big deal. If the customer feels there is some sort of relationship, then the customer is going to have a stronger customer sat and a stronger customer sat leads to additional purchases, which all combine for higher renewal rates. On top of that, if an actual employee is going to reach out or meet with the customer, a summary sheet can be provided with suggestions for ice breaking topics and likes/dislikes to help the conversation move quickly..

Bottom line, my idea is all about scaling the connection. People like doing business with people they like. With our Proactive Relationship Intelligence,  they will be more likely to like us.

Let’s talk. Now I am even more excited than I was…😊

BTW…seeing that they got $20+m in series A is very encouraging to me; I truly think I am on to something. My concept can already help with direct connections, scaling community, sales follow-up, customer service, social media dialogue, HR engagement, education (k-12, higher ed, and adult), and more, so developing a use case around the BDR is not a big leap. We could be a plug in module to make current investment even better.

I think it went really well with Greg; he even laughed at some of my dumb jokes. 😊

The deck was a recommendation from one of my Senior VPs at Microsoft. He recommended that om addition to always keeping your resume up-to-date, you should always have a walking deck ready to provide some personal context beyond the bullet points in a normal resume.

I have a call with Keith at 5:30pm tonight and am very hopeful he wants to move forward with me in this role.

---Steve

From: Robison, Christina <crobison@lwsd.org>
Sent: Thursday, August 15, 2019 2:21:36 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: EHS is looking for AVID Volunteers

[cid:image001.jpg@01D55374.2B13FE60]



Dear Steve Winfield,
You're receiving this email because you're an approved volunteer for Eastlake High School.
As you may know, LWSD has been using a program called AVID (Advancement Via Individual Determination)<https://www.avid.org/what-avid-is> at many of our middle schools and high schools for the last few years. For this program to be successful we need a lot of volunteers to assist our students.
Eastlake High School is looking for some more volunteers to help with this program. Volunteers work with a small group of students once a week for one class period.
If you are interested, we will be having several information sessions to learn more about becoming an AVID mentor.
Information Sessions (all at LWSD Resource Center located at 16250 NE 74th Street, Redmond, WA 98052):
August 19            1:00-2:00 p.m.          Hughes Room
August 21            6:00-7:00 p.m.       Sammamish Room
August 27            3:30-4:30 p.m.        Sammamish Room
Please let me know if you would like to come to an information session.
Thank you for volunteering your valuable time to LWSD!

Chris Robison

LWSD Volunteer Program Supervisor

Lake Washington School District

P.O. Box 97039

Redmond, WA 98073-9739

425 936-1270

From: Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com>
Sent: Tuesday, July 3, 2018 1:29:00 PM
To: Steve Winfield
Subject: Re: We gave it our best

Are you busy watching the game?

Thanks,
Elizabeth


On Jul 3, 2018, at 8:56 AM, Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:

Thank you for this. It means a lot to me.

I would like to process it for a bit before I respond.

---Steve

Get Outlook for iOS<https://eur03.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Faka.ms%2Fo0ukef&data=02%7C01%7C%7Cb72cf9fb0de04118602f08d5e0fd914c%7C84df9e7fe9f640afb435aaaaaaaaaaaa%7C1%7C0%7C636662302018428149&sdata=mbcYJiUbgZnzdVyOVmKmtThkxk3mTIOru9zpTzBojwg%3D&reserved=0>
________________________________
From: Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com<mailto:elidunc@hotmail.com>>
Sent: Tuesday, July 3, 2018 6:03:57 AM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: We gave it our best


We really did. We gave ourselves so many chances. Tons of chances. We tried for two years. For two counselors. Lots of books. Gottman.

We tried. We for sure tried. Yes? I think we can agree on that.

And we’ve both learned sooooo much. And we both are so much better for it. Minus the collateral damage to our hearts. Not meaning to speak for you.

We’ve grown so much.

This is the right thing to do. We didn’t work out. I am still heartbroken. And I still grieve.

But we are not together for a reason. And those reasons are probably still there. No judgment on either of us. I’m not implying anything by this email.

Other than to say, yes I miss you and could so easily enter your orbit in whatever way is possible.

But I don’t think that’s good for either of us. Yes, the moments would be undeniable. But I can’t do the roller coaster. I see that drop coming and I can’t do it. This has been absolute hell just to get to this point.

I’m wanting to put my heart and energy into a bright future.

You moving to SF gives you a fresh start. A bright future. Having me in it doesn’t.

For me, tangling with you again would be me entering a situation where you will be mostly absent. I can’t do it. I’m ready to not be alone. I’m not doing long distance.

You have to go to SF. Maybe tangling with me again eases you into this ginormous huge next step in your life that you’re taking.

I only spoke to you yesterday bc you’re leaving. It was a safe guard measure.

I’m drawn to you even against all logic. So you going to SF is a good thing.

We were passion but not logic.

You going to SF on your own and with out support from your new job. Being an empty nester now (which is highly desirable on dating apps I suspect!). Huge changes in your life. Maybe you’re wanting to see me before you go and to have me visit you there is offering you some familiarity. Continuity. Comfort.

I just don’t see the upside for me though. Just a few fun times before you go? A few visits here and there? I loved you with all my heart. I still think you’re adorable. But that’s not enough reason to get attached to you all over again.

I cannot believe you are leaving. It has not sunk in yet. Like you said, the finality.

Btw, did you know Emma sent me a graduation invite? It occurs to me you might not have known that. I was happy for her. And you. Congratulations to you both actually.

And I renewed my eagles membership. Did you?

I think we need to listen to our counselors.


Thanks,
Elizabeth
subject: Re: We gave it our best

body:
I think that is a good idea.

Both kids want to have a grandfather in their lives and we're very pleased you are showing interest in them.

-----

I was trying to figure out how to respond to some of what you said in your email, as I categorically disagree with some of your comments.

I was 10 years old when the divorce happened. 10!!! Did you really expect a 10 year old to reach out and make you feel wanted? You were the grown up and I was your kid. You should have asked for visitation rights as part of the divorce. You didn't. You quit your job as a parent. You were a grown up. You made a choice. You wanted freedom from fatherhood. All of my friends with divorced parents had their fathers still in their lives to some extent. Their dads made a point to see them even once a month, the dads called, and the dads were interested in their kid's life. With the divorce, you disappeared. I saw you twice between 10 years of age and 17. Once was when you were on a layover at DFW airport and the other time was when I made my mom arrange an afternoon with you down in Houston when she was there at a contest. Heck, you saw the Bowens more than me!!! There were times I saw you walking into their house or your car parked in front. Each time I  expected you to come visit afterwards, but you never did. I wanted a dad in my life; you chose not to be involved.

As a grown up, I tried numerous times to get you back into my life and the lives of the kids. I invited you to come and visit when you were going to be nearby in the RV, flew you up to visit a few times when the kids were younger, offered to send you pictures, wanted you to be connected online to them as they grew up, called periodically, but it was always a one way street and you almost always made me feel like you would only get involved if it was convenient and it didn't get in the way of visiting your friends, your siblings, or Sadie's kids. Plus, you always waited for me to engage you as opposed to you engaging with us.

You are upset that you weren't made to feel special. You are the dad!!! When did you make me feel special? Why didn't you want to be in my life? Why didn't you want to know about me? Why weren't you interested in my job? My marriage? My kids?

The fact that you abandoned me at 10 had been a frequent topic of conversation in my therapy. Now, I get to add that YOU blame ME for my own abandonment!!! I was a kid!!! You were a grown up!!! You were my father!!! How in the world can you seriously feel that the reason we have never been close is all my fault and not really yours at all?

That's crazy to me.

However, this is more about doing something for my kids and not about you and me.

-----
I am very pleased that Emma's graduation announcement may have changed something in the relationship between you and me and my kids. I hope it continues. I would like it and know the kids would love it.

But understand, if you get into their lives now, they will expect you to stay involved. Send them a text or an email every once in a while. Heck, give them a call once a month and ask how things are going. Show interest in what they are doing. Show interest in their life. If you do that, you will find that they will reciprocate.

Here is their information :

Chad.Winfield@hotmail.con
425-753-7576
September 4, 1996

EmmaWinfield@live.com
‭425-588-6330
May 27, 2000
‬

From: Floyd Winfield <fwinfield72@gmail.com>
Sent: Sunday, July 22, 2018 7:47:01 AM
To: Steve Winfield-Mobile
Subject: Funds for Emma and Chad

Steve,

I made a proposal last week and asked you to get back to me, but that's neither here nor there now.  I am going to send each of your kids $3000 to use as a base for the​ir finances, along with a note about how to "control the use of a credit card and not let the credit card, and credit card debt, control you."

The following is the note I plan on sending when I arrange for the checks:
(Between the lines.)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Many people, when they get some money, along with a credit card, think that they have an endless supply of money.  Credit card companies love this kind
of thinking because they will give you as much "credit" as you want.  The only
problem is, if you aren't careful, you wind up owing more than you have, at
a 20% plus interest rate and it is almost impossible to get "caught up!"

I would suggest that you take this $3000, open a bank account in your name,
and order two credit cards in your name.  Use one credit card for all your recurring monthly charges such as rent, utilities, telephone, car payment and any other "monthly" or occasional charge that simply must be made.  This card should be "tucked away" in your,purse, or wallet, and never used for random, day to day charges.

The other credit card should be used for any "random" charges such as movies, fast food, entertainment and any other charge that you have from day to day.  With this method, you will keep your necessary monthly charges separate from your random charges and you can see how much you are spending "out of pocket."  I would also suggest that you use your "random" card for anything over $5.00.  I start with some $20.00 bills in my wallet and a
$10, $5 and five ones in my pocket.  You can use this money for anything less
than $5.00.  This method will provide you with a record of all your day to day
expenses.

I would further suggest that when the credit card payment is due, you should
pay it off in its' entirety.  By doing this, you can evaluate your financial status
very easily.  By using the "base line" of $3000 as a comparison to your "bank
balance" you will know if you are spending more or less than your income.  If
the bank balance is more than $3000, you are spending less than you are
receiving.  It the balance is less, you are over-spending and since you have a
record of your random spending, you can find some way to adjust.

Using this method and by arranging "Auto-Pay" for all your monthly charges, including the credit card bills, you will not have to worry about monthly bills
and you will never have a "Late Payment."  You should still check all your
monthly charges on your credit card bill to make sure that everything is okay!  With this method, my "Credit Report" is running at 792 to 798 every month.  It did drop to 750, which is still higher than most people in this country, when my home insurance policy lapsed.  I thought I had it set up for auto-pay but found out that the insurance company does not use credit cards for auto renewal...only drafts on a bank  account, which I now have set up for next year!

So...use this money to begin life as a debt-free person and by monitoring the
performance on a month to month basis, you can remain debt free.  As I told
your father, I am doing this to kind of make up for not doing more for he and
your two uncles when they were in college.

I want you two to have as highly enjoyable a college career as possible
without having to worry too much about your finances.

If you have any questions about my suggestions, please feel free to call me.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you approve of the $3000 and the note, I will arrange for the checks to be
sent in the next few days.  If you do not approve, I will send each of your
kids $1000 and you are all on your own and I will totally back out and return
to my "non-person" status.  I am just trying to make your kids college lives as
enjoyable as I can.  I have too much experience in living when you never know
when you are going to be faced with a debt and not knowing how in hell you are going to pay it!

Waiting to hear from you!

Dad

body:
☺

I do like your feet…but I don’t want you to think I am a super weirdo about it.

So, if you don’t mind teasing me with pictures of your shoes/barefeet or putting your feet up on my lap, then great.

If you want me to tone it down, I can easily do it as it is not a must-have or a deal-breaker.

Make sense?

subject: Channel Manager in Texas [reply]

body:
Hi Linda,

I went through the SalesQB process but haven't received any follow-up, so I'm guessing I didn't make the cut. I get it — on paper, my resume looks like enterprise channel sales, not fractional sales management. But I think that's exactly why I'd be great at this.

Here's the thing: I've spent 25 years exceeding quotas by working with sales teams I had zero authority over. As a channel leader, my job was to walk into a partner's office, understand their customers, their process, their goals...and then help their salespeople sell more effectively. No direct reports. No control over their comp plans. Just influence, enablement, and trust.

Sound familiar? That's the SalesQB model.

The traditional sales leader you might be considering is used to managing one team at a time. I've spent my entire career juggling multiple partners simultaneously, each with different verticals, different sales motions, different personalities. Keeping them segmented in my head and my process while making sure each one gets what they need to win? That's not something I'll have to learn. I've been doing it for two decades.

I also bring range. Eleven years at Microsoft working with global enterprise partners. Startups like Trustifi and Lookout where I built channel programs from scratch. Cybersecurity companies like Kroll and Cybereason. My own consulting business I've kept running on the side for 12 years. I've worked with companies of every size, from Fortune 500 to five-person shops. And my first career as a teacher and principal in Texas taught me how to read a room, adjust on the fly, and meet people where they are...skills I use every single day.

Here's my ask: Give me 15 minutes with someone on your team. If I haven't convinced them in that time that I'd be great at this...not just good, but great...I'll happily move on. But I'm confident that after 15 minutes, they'll want to keep talking.

Looking forward to the conversation.

Steve

425-753-8897
swinfield@hotmail.com

If selected for next-round interviewing, you will receive an email with suggested dates/times for your 30-minute call with the HR Director. If you do not receive an invitation to interview in the next 3 to 5 business days, it means you were not selected.



Again, you should expect to receive notification within 3-5 business days from the date of this email. If by chance you are not invited to interview, do not feel discouraged. We have thousands applying every month with very few spots in each territory.



Should you make it to the end of the interview process, I will be speaking with you then.



Take care,



Laurie Davis

Subject: New Responder Assets

Hi all,

I’m pleased to share two new key assets for our Kroll Responder MDR service:


  1.  [​pptx icon]  Updated Sales Deck<https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/ap/p-59584e83/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fduffandphelps.sharepoint.com%2F%3Ap%3A%2Fs%2Fko_consulting%2FCyberIR%2Fcyberpresents%2FET7pWh5Qvk1MsNLYNHUxeroBltVxkhMkuNWtvR7eB7LqGw&data=05%7C01%7C%7C22a93ba943014e8c4d7208dae4c79f31%7C84df9e7fe9f640afb435aaaaaaaaaaaa%7C1%7C0%7C638073843261575008%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=A%2BdLmu7C0mEiLTJGXth0BxbEm7LNrACZOoqGJQcZ8c8%3D&reserved=0> – Please download and save as a copy to edit. This version includes a more polished layout/design and includes some key areas that were missing from the previous version. This is not intended to completely replace what you’re currently using but rather provide more options and a useful template that you can take to include in your pitch. I would however encourage to use what you can from this deck as this has been laid out to improve previous versions and provide a level of consistency in our messaging and positioning. This deck follows a recognized flow of:

1) laying out the ‘undeniable change’ in the industry that the prospect must keep up with

2) outline where they need to get to succeed in this new era

3) outlining how Kroll is already enabling clients to achieve this

4) explaining the benefits and differentiators of our approach and

5) summarizing our value so they go away remembering key attributes.



  1.  [​docx icon]  Positioning Guide<https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/ap/w-59584e83/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fduffandphelpseur.sharepoint.com%2F%3Aw%3A%2Fs%2FProductStrategyManagement%2FEcVkQ9MEFJFEnt43PYzYrpgBGvjbUppeOPfQ-kPuj2MC0Q%3Fe%3DyQarl2&data=05%7C01%7C%7C22a93ba943014e8c4d7208dae4c79f31%7C84df9e7fe9f640afb435aaaaaaaaaaaa%7C1%7C0%7C638073843261731232%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=y3aN9ASrzWQib7E5AV%2B4e9QIyiDDQcNrs3F105YCrB0%3D&reserved=0> – This document is for internal use as a resource for understanding our go-to-market positioning, which aims to:

  *   enable customer-facing teams to use our core messaging and value statements in conversations and pitches,
  *   ensure we have a consistent voice when speaking to customers and prospects
  *   provide direction on our ideal target profile
  *   guide how we differentiate from competitors

These are not static, but rather living documents that we will continue to tweak as the market and our offering evolves.

Regards,
_____________________________________________
Ben Chant
Senior Manager Customer Experience, Cyber Risk

Subject: RE: Mimecast?

I like Mimecast a lot. The reasons we went with ProofPoint vs Mimecast were three-fold:


  1.  I had a really good contact at PP that set us up with distributor status. PP has very few distributors and we’re one of them. I did not have a contact at Mimecast to discuss distribution options. I’ve actually never had a contact at Mimecast to work with.
  2.  McAfee was bailing on the mail filtering business and PP had done a deal with McAfee to take over their old clients. We ended up getting a ton of old McAfee clients transitioned to PP in a short period of time. This poured a ton of gas on our PP fire.
  3.  We ended up getting really awesome pricing from PP and scooped up a lot of business because we are really good at the technology and have killer pricing. Good combo.

Not to toot our own horn too much but we are really good at selling mail filtering. We have a high level of understanding of how to setup the DNS, how to do migrations, how to make the settings work to achieve a high level of filtering, setup encryption, etc. We’ve just gotten really good at it. I genuinely believe, and I’m not trying to brag, that we are the best PP distributor in the country in terms of execution and customer satisfaction. It’s my belief that nobody migrates new mail filtering customers better than us and we have a smooth setup to handle the monthly billing (this is critical). If you needed a go-to distributor for Mimecast, we could be that for you. And, I’d love to work with you again because we have good chemistry.

I hope you get the gig and we get a chance to sell for you. 😊 We’d crush it!!

Aside from of our situation, Mimecast is a good company with good technology. I could see you working there for a long time. The mail filtering business in general is a growing business, specifically with encryption and archiving. It’s a way better job opportunity than Comodo, for example.

Nathan

From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Sent: Saturday, April 7, 2018 12:45 PM
To: Nathan Ware <Nathan@rainnetworks.com>
Subject: Mimecast?

Hi Nathan...

I hope you are doing well.

Mimecast is recruiting me for their NW region channel lead role.

I have some familiarity with the company and the technology, but value your opinion.

What do you think of them? Why did you choose ProofPoint instead of Mimecast?

---Steve

Your flight on Wednesday, December 23 is available for check-in.
We've Got You Covered
As always, your safety is our top priority. That’s why you’ll experience a new standard of care on your upcoming trip. Our Delta CareStandard<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813dcaa3366bb336f702555d00bab395b9dd97d6bf53b5c2d65761274b552931d564ee2736fe011098b49c81460e03d49e79d5e367ab2085f9c> focuses on keeping surfaces clean, giving you more space, and offering safer service and personal care at every point in your journey.

Stay In Control Of Your Travel Plans
We’re making it easier to make changes yourself on delta.com and the Fly Delta app. You can cancel or modify your itinerary via MyTrips<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813f399e1f3fc1da9d1d0cf95af9cba098c41b3ef67a1a5b5eaa17a6b6cfceeb31fdf792e17b7d65f49f0dd81dccf1d5130e05cc21168d3b94d> prior to departure without any change fees. This helps ensure those traveling can be seated further apart while also meeting weight and balance guidelines. You can also check the seat map on MyTrips<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813f399e1f3fc1da9d1d0cf95af9cba098c41b3ef67a1a5b5eaa17a6b6cfceeb31fdf792e17b7d65f49f0dd81dccf1d5130e05cc21168d3b94d> prior to departure to see if seats adjacent to yours are occupied or available for sale.

Confirm You're Healthy To Fly
At check-in, prepare to commit to wearing a mask during your trip and acknowledge that you have not experienced symptoms and/or been exposed or tested positive for COVID-19 in the past 14 days. More information.<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813c229a68623e8b0538799f9bb18da4cec598d6ba34a85d56fcf323d06117f5e9b8b52e9ecdf93fff89af9b717c4442d0d9f47c33032328ca8>

We also recommend packing your own food items, especially on longer flights, since many airport offerings are limited during this time. Please be sure to review TSA guidelines<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813ff2fb9689f07dd3b6e563dbe650fe2b841ecfa4edc6975ff732f6e0d2e8ef2282f9f6aa2262ea96fcaa3ceb862865f4b55626f2f72ed26e8> before bringing food through security checkpoints.
YOUR PRE-TRIP CHECKLIST FOR EASIER TRAVEL:

JOIN SKYMILES® - start earning miles that don't expire and can take you to more than 1,000 destinations. In addition, receive important information about your trip whenever you fly. Join today for free >><https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=39d8de2d880e206be973e09cf824eb495f36109b2f369553392823b905476856deaec13e4c9894727bc20603b5c1dee949739066d54e6ef3d3f65f8aaf93ced9>

DOWNLOAD THE FLY DELTA APP – during this time, flight schedules can change quickly, so we strongly encourage you to download the Fly Delta app before you leave for the airport. With real-time push notifications sent to your mobile device, you’ll never miss an update, and you can easily check your flight status under the "My Trips" tab. Download Now >><https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=39d8de2d880e206bff1f50d8bfa5f3f2dd1c30db8d5a72eca39d8c16c94d18ab23581905685e990a8f79562857d8a95cc18c6d58e38e03fae2f332a724f919e0>

CALCULATE YOUR BAGGAGE ESTIMATE  – use the baggage calculator to get an estimate of the baggage allowance and fees for you upcoming trip. Visit the My Trips<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=39d8de2d880e206bd71c1fdcfed9134f8e0228005f3c9c409791a7dd20574e90c4314127301e9018539418a9f917544f98457866ec1d2b76548e453863a75443> page and select an upcoming trip.

VISIT OUR NEED HELP PAGE – get all your travel questions answered with information on self-service tools, baggage, SkyMiles, and more. >><https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=39d8de2d880e206b6ed423d5309134085c6263834fbd69b1db87ca6039f74b2ae2cb1287819d7eb2ffd39e2fcf62a81ae3fde98c7b0bec3c00634351429c8c22>

Have a great trip, and thank you for choosing Delta.
Trip One
Wednesday
December 23     DEPART  ARRIVE  SEAT
Delta 631
Los Angeles, California
7:30 am         Mexico City
1:10 pm         33A
Trip Two
Thursday
January 7       DEPART  ARRIVE  SEAT
Delta 630
Mexico City
4:00 pm         Los Angeles, California
6:09 pm         30F
GOVERNMENT-ISSUED COVID-19 TRAVEL RESTRICTIONS

Several countries/states have issued travel mandates that may affect your trip. We strongly encourage all customers to review the COVID-19 Travel Restrictions<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e081379c97502ca66e2091ced34a035990349bb5d708d0c8251f767675c499eaf6562b15d1a5c4a68da79b2c4124b29ed0999a54aa8d7ba5df483> before arriving at the airport. You may check your eligibility to change or cancel your flight here<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e08139f1e50d43b2a422ed70f3f336d498232fe033338561b2770f0bb91287cae7a5b63217b5e006191d32e1f11c250dec7cca26d48cebe884b55>.
MASKS REQUIRED FOR EVERYONE'S SAFETY

Per CDC recommendations, all employees and customers are required to wear masks or face coverings throughout the travel journey. Customers requiring medical-based exemptions for not wearing face coverings or masks should be prepared to complete a clearance to fly process prior to departure at the airport. Learn More ><https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=db8e5db9a92e0813f3e693499b9c9b7b345552ff142582f12c981bbfa6c1667a77d1a07d66d14c295e524607fa92e84faf7a80d2b1059596bafc016ee79315ca>
LAX BAG DROP UPDATE

Please proceed to the bag drop area in Terminal 2 even if you're departing from a different terminal. If you have carry-on bags only, you may proceed directly to your departure terminal.
SAVE TIME BY COMPLETING THE MEXICAN IMMIGRATION FORM (FMM) AHEAD OF TIME

Passengers traveling to or connecting in Mexico who don't have a Mexican passport can expedite the immigration process by completing the Mexican immigration form (FMM)<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=486973ec7f1b3bbebc7bcda2b792f66e5d55f4186bd9e1091a77123044b31bf8e1b87b2b38a4e5782d6bbf22ac1f60868d8802c0bdb17e4571a2d342911ca2b5>. Just print it out and bring it with you on your day of travel.
MODIFICATIONS TO DELTA SKY CLUB® EXPERIENCE AT LAX FOR GUESTS WITH ACCESS

While the majority of our Delta Sky Club network remains open, we are temporarily consolidating Club operations. Keeping your safety top of mind, we are focused on the Delta CareStandardSM which includes keeping surfaces clean and disinfected. Due to current California state and LA County restrictions, no alcoholic beverages may be served at this time. Additionally, food packaged to go may be carried out, but may not be consumed inside the Club. Hours may also vary during this time. See all the ways we are working to ensure safety and social distancing at our Clubs here<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=98c00717089a1ea6ee023fddc155a0a4bf92cb695adb505cfa422457040c3865a1ac12591b19c59cf9923e5ef977978e986a994bd391f9c99951ad1506e4fa3f>.
RESTRICTED HAZARDOUS ITEMS

To ensure the safety of our customers and employees, Delta does not accept smart bags. Smart bags with non-removable lithium-ion batteries will not be permitted as carry-on or checked baggage on any Delta mainline or Delta Connection flight. For more information, please visit our News Hub<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=9516ae874e08c7874f2777dcdae9c43605c8c34f5dee547a82dcb65a9fbb6dfc3f8656adefe4fa161850d4c25f5b4dad8a44ab0126d3e4944febcb411c7f08fd>.

All damaged, defective or recalled lithium batteries, including lithium powered self-balancing transportation devices are not permitted as carry-on or checked baggage.

Spare batteries for other devices, fuel cells, and e-cigarettes are permitted in carry-on baggage only. If your carry-on bag contains these items and is gate checked, they must be removed and carried in the cabin. Further information and specific guidelines regarding restricted items can be found here<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605b5aaef332e0917225a0dff5c867cd68e940bac13f106729cc749ecdee39cc9a0bd944ea9f6efcbbbe47ab5807aeee25dc65703123e04c1ab>.

Conditions of Carriage

Air transportation on Delta and the Delta Connection® carriers is subject to Delta's conditions of carriage<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605c51b2d70716bbb2369a898739ba4b93e2820b658c22e54f5b788af7565fc3e05c71a14d1d52d583a25aff6d268b0e2e414d4ca664436270a>. They include terms governing for example:

• Limits on our liability<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605e571d852bfcc696d1c458afde6819c18e056f7d1206f228dfcd3b4cf325724a22c9bbdcfa7b8af04ddb2e8e9454fa7ed028df1c243fae22b> for personal injury or death of passengers, and for loss, damage of delay of goods and baggage.
• Claim restrictions<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605f438bd898cb70c2f04ee1b69bb3925d10dad14ac74d5aec254041d9bbbbf7bc0210018f8592ed493e1a74b2e3071c43e32f87762e54705b0> including time periods within which you must file a claim or bring action against us.
• Our right to change terms<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b826053e7e6cf77caca00b365aaf02a47e2a7c99831b13d0b283f64f77f11eebe78525c0c54a2c731532a9c6d9fff54692e6e482840751a18e7c4c> of the contract.
• Check-in requirements<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605d15f28d5130313310e4fc31252a1fea082ac4cd889031fdc4ccf7ef358dd097805f076c27589d84ee76bdb86849c103f678e49d4d0e36bef> and other rules established when we may refuse carriage<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b826059d24da6fb6dc277e4b374376d620de163791bbf8a88fe97ebc525591589dc62b2cf42da0fcca0b1c53eb17a7ffbb6c14b770b61b28b7ab0d>.
• Our rights and limits of our liability for delay or failure to perform service<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b82605cd73fb74aaed37db5e8485d3c0049876f073b2a77fcddd565ac43d0775a8dd4730b383bd0623fa7ac507f07cd125772a210d30514eb09d5c> including schedule change, substitution of alternative air carriers or aircraft, and rerouting.
• Our policy on overbooking flights<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b826055ff8fc15fdf21df904f38851ceed137fdba37267a66b87b1802368960e2131d55f49a54315b8e2a28814acce1be26b36afe336a0612d6490>, and your rights if we deny you boarding due to an oversold flight.

Advice To Passengers On Limitations Of Liability

For international travel to or from another country, airline liability is governed by the Montreal Convention or the Warsaw Convention. There are no financial limits for death or bodily injury, and the airline may make an advance payment to cover immediate economic needs. The airline's liability for passenger delay, and for loss, delay or damage to baggage may be limited by the Conventions. In case of baggage, the liability limit is 1131 Special Drawing Rights (approximately US $1787) per passenger for international travel. The baggage limit is US $3,500 per ticketed passenger for US domestic travel. These baggage liability limitations apply unless you declare a higher valuation and pay additional charges at check-in. In case of international travel, any action in court to claim damages must be brought within two years from the date of the actual or scheduled arrival of the aircraft; and, in case of baggage claims, written notice to the carrier must be made within 7 days of the receipt of checked baggage in case of damage, and, in case of delay, within 21 days from the date on which it was placed at the disposal of the passenger. If your journey also involves carriage by other airlines, you should contact them for information on their limits of liability. See the notice with your tickets or consult your airline or travel agent for further information.

Email Subscription

You are currently subscribed to receive Delta Messenger Notifications via email at SWINFIELD@HOTMAIL.COM. If you would like to take advantage of other Delta email programs featuring special fares, promotions and information, please visit delta.com/emailprograms<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=75ab8189b0b826053d4a5f837f4fa07581683fde32a82a30c8ddfcc0a491317490fd53f204e0a0021634c1fd408b35bfc454c6f50fe4cabf7b8c6f425a33838d>

Privacy Policy

Your privacy is important to us. Please review our Privacy Policy<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=4ed74dfda49e97f86df57c7234e31fa057a11d65e183e828d30943671730243e1d22b725b7d25ebe9a188a9e1767d2f4289e3efeb66d70107f67e690fff601a0>.

Effective December 15, 2019, the Canada Air Passenger Protection Regulations may provide additional protections to passengers traveling to or from Canada:

If you are denied boarding, your flight is cancelled or delayed for at least two hours, or your baggage is lost or damaged, you may be entitled to certain standards of treatment and compensation under the Air Passenger Protection Regulations. For more information about your passenger rights please contact your air carrier (www.delta.com/appr<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=4ed74dfda49e97f80c96321df0c51933003e4c06a49ca79f8bcf8dedcf4e796712dc4659287380f031c6ebfc7c71290505f71e2c8f0c0288b84442513123e4ae>) or visit the Canadian Transportation Agency’s website.

Si l’embarquement vous est refusé, ou si votre vol est annulé ou retardé d’au moins deux heures ou si vos bagages sont perdus ou endommagés, vous pourriez avoir droit au titre du Règlement sur la protection des passagers aériens, à certains avantages au titre des normes de traitement applicables et à une indemnité. Pour de plus amples renseignements sur vos droits, veuillez communiquer avec votre transporteur aérien ( www.delta.com/appr<https://click.t.delta.com/u/?qs=4ed74dfda49e97f8fed067c8d9420943b8be9eb50a97c6b52c8bf002dbddfd07a052db422f6e9d4d5d4291bf84e346d8988c9a40529b1d08e940b27512dbc027>) ou visiter le site Web de l’Office des transports du Canada.

Copyright Information

Thank you. I look forward to hearing from you.

As an FYI, I had another company question my “job hopping” so I want to give you some quick data points. The jobs in GREEN are the ones I left voluntarily and on good terms. The ones in RED are the ones in which I had no choice.:


  *   Cybereason – Left as part of 17% RiF globally and 77% of my direct team.
  *   Trustifi – Left when recruited by VP from Lookout to help start Alliance team at Cybereason with a 50% raise.
  *   TrustArc – Left when executive team decided to keep Direct Sales team to compete openly with channel partners.
  *   Lookout – Left when my position was RiF’d as part of a change in channel direction from regional SIs to Telcos.
  *   SoftwareONE – Left when recruited by my director from Kaspersky to return to the channel at Lookout.
  *   Kaspersky Lab – Left when my position was RiF’d as part of a sales reorg. They moved the headcount from Seattle to Philadelphia.
  *   M&A Computers – Left because the project I was hired for was completed.

body:
Any update. It is now Tuesday and still nothing.
________________________________
From: Customer Service <sales@pokerchips.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 22, 2023 12:05:38 PM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: sales@pokerchips.com

Hello,

Thanks for reaching out about your order!

I'm very sorry for the delays in the production of your order. We had a very large influx of orders recently that have caused some delays across the board for my production team. We are producing everyone's order as fast as possible.

Your order is scheduled for production on Friday, 3/24. Orders usually ship out the same day they're created and an automated tracking information email will be sent to you once our shipping department has created a FedEx label for your package.

With the FedEx 2Day Delivery option, you should have your chips in hand early next week, most likely on Tuesday.

Very Best,
-Mike
Customer Service

On Wed, Mar 22, 2023 at 2:00 PM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
Hi. My order says complete, but I never even received a tracking number let alone the actual chips.

Can you let me not what is happening?

---Steve

425-753-8897
Swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:Swinfield@hotmail.com>
subject: Re: sales@pokerchips.com

From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>
Sent: Wednesday, October 16, 2024 6:19 AM
To: DiZoglio, Deb <Deb.DiZoglio@kroll.com<mailto:Deb.DiZoglio@kroll.com>>
Cc: Carter, Keith <Keith.Carter@kroll.com<mailto:Keith.Carter@kroll.com>>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] Re: Your Departure Information: Steve Winfield



Hi...



Can you tell me if I will receive my money on the 23rd or the end of the month?



---Steve

________________________________

From: DiZoglio, Deb <Deb.DiZoglio@kroll.com<mailto:Deb.DiZoglio@kroll.com>>
Sent: Thursday, October 10, 2024 4:00 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com> <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>
Cc: Carter, Keith <Keith.Carter@kroll.com<mailto:Keith.Carter@kroll.com>>
Subject: Your Departure Information: Steve Winfield



Dear Steve,

I would like to go over a few details with you regarding your departure on Wednesday, October 23, 2024.  Attached is your Separation Agreement, please review, should you choose, sign, and return to my attention; once returned I will process the paperwork.



Someone from the IT department will contact you to coordinate the return of any company property (i.e., laptop, ID card, etc.). You will need to submit the completed exit checklist attached to krollforyou@kroll.com<mailto:krollforyou@kroll.com>.

A list of People Team contacts is included in the attached packet for your reference.



Here are a few reminders for you:

  *   Personal information

o   The personal email address we have on file is swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com> . Let us know if this is incorrect.

  *   Final Pay & Vacation Payout

     *   On the next regularly scheduled pay date following your separation date, or sooner if state or local law requires, you will receive a check for all unpaid wages earned through your separation date and any accrued, but unused vacation.
     *   You will be paid out any unused hours of vacation.

  *   Benefits & Compensation

     *   Certain health & welfare benefits, if you participate, will continue for the remainder of the month in which you terminate. See attached Separation Information and Benefits Packet..
     *   COBRA benefits information will be sent to your home address approximately 14 days from your benefits termination date. If you have benefits questions, please contact Benefits at HC.Benefits@kroll.com<mailto:HC.Benefits@kroll.com>.
     *   If you would like to convert Life Insurance Plans into a personal plan, please review the attached Unum brochure.
     *   If you have a Kroll Savings Plan balance, update your email address (to replace your Kroll email), and review your contact information at NewportGroup.com prior to your last day. You may have the option of keeping your balance with the plan. Alternatively, you may request a distribution by completing the attached distribution form and returning it via email or regular mail to the attention of HC.Benefits@kroll.com<mailto:HC.Benefits@kroll.com> or Attn: Benefits Department at 300 Headquarter Plaza, East Tower, Floor 12, Morristown, NJ 07960.
     *   If you have Kroll Restricted Cash and have any questions, please contact Lauren Stevenson, 212-450-2867.

  *   Finance

     *   W2s are typically issued in January of the following calendar year. Please make sure to inform us of any address changes no later than December 31st to ensure your tax forms are properly mailed.
     *   Your corporate card will be canceled on the day of your departure. You will be responsible for paying off your Amex balance, if applicable.

  *   Confidential Information & Company Property

     *   All physical and electronic Kroll property and records in your possession or control must be returned to the IT department on your last day. All company and client information remains confidential even after you leave the Company.
     *   You should not wipe any company information or data from your phone, laptop, or other electronic devices. Personal files saved on the Company phone or laptop will not be retrievable once returned.
     *   Please note you will be held to the terms regarding post-employment obligations outlined in your offer letter, including Confidentiality, Non-Solicitation and Proprietary Rights, if applicable.
     *   Cyber Risk employees are not permitted to port out of Kroll Cyber issued mobile numbers.



Please note that it is not appropriate to send goodbye e-mails to large groups, including your entire practice. Instead, a contact information e-mail can be sent out to close friends and colleagues.

If you have questions, please let me know.





Regards,



_____________________________________________

Deborah DiZoglio

People Business Partner II, Cyber Risk



T +1 617-334-3949 | M +1 617-334-3949

To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: Registration/Living with Aunt



Hello -



To enroll William at Foothill High School please complete all steps below. Once all requested documents have been emailed to me, I will arrange a Zoom meeting with his counselor to complete enrollment. For reference, William's student ID will remain the same: 83729


New Student Enrollment Checklist:

  *   Registration Form <https://1.cdn.edl.io/gsQq1PUfHGMWR1wvJEyWsp2VxoCZehgmEAvRVbIaONTkp8e0.pdf> (paper portion - also required)
  *   Affidavit of residency for student alone (attached) - this must be signed by you and his aunt. His aunt must initial all of the 6 spaces on the bottom portion
  *   2 proofs of residency in his aunt's name (utility statements)
  *   Birth certificate or passport
  *   Official immunizations
  *   Transcript from prior school
  *   Course selection form. The Academic Program Guide 2021-2022<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1AmfEBis1JZoWfVivt68Un8GzFsirzpur354zSvHZztU/preview> may help with selection.

        *   12th Grade Course Selection Worksheet<https://drive.google.com/file/d/1k-w4vE1kNIqDlemoa1Q1PsGA0S_0oYeU/view>

  *   IEP or 504 if applicable
  *   Request for student records, attached


Please let me know if I can answer any questions.


Sincerely,

Janet Burnett

Hello Mrs. Gregory…

Last year, when we moved into the district from Mexico, Andrew had been placed in Mrs. Bell’s 5th grade class (against my strong recommendation). He stayed in that class until mid-February when he was moved into Ms. Kantor’s 4th grade class.

This move occurred right before the trip to Pigeon Point, so he was not able to go. Unfortunately, had already paid the $340 fee for the trip as well as for a shirt and a sweatshirt we never received.

We were told last year that the $340 would be carried over for the trip this year.

So, for Andrew to go this year, we should only have to pay the remaining $60.

Let me know if we need to have a call to discuss this.

Thanks.

---Steve

425-753-8897

body:
I don’t know how to respond to your opinions. I am confused by them. I think back to our relationship and the happiness we had and struggle to figure out what the things are about me that you just can’t live with. Even though I have repeatedly told you and sworn that I am loyal and have never cheated and never would, you refuse to believe me. You have created this false narrative in your mind that I hope someday you realize. It will be sad that when you reach heaven and get to see back on your life and realize how badly you misconstrued things and falsely accused me. This misperception and mistrust has made it where we are missing out on having an absolutely amazing life together.

Also, I think back to our major arguments and pretty much every one of them occurred when there was alcohol involved. When we were completely sober, we rarely if ever fought.

So, I struggle to understand.

And as far as your comments about not being sad or minimally sad, I already told you that I think of you daily. I wish you were with me. I wish we were together. I miss your voice. Your laugh. Your touch. Your smell. Your kiss.

You have made it very clear that you have no interest in ever really discussing us again, even the picture you sent this morning says you want to forget me.

How do I respond?

I think you are amazing and I don’t think I will ever have the same level of happiness as I had with you.

---Steve

From: Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com>
Sent: Sunday, July 29, 2018 12:17 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com
Subject: Re: Sigh

So I want to let you know that by you not acknowledging my opinions repeatedly you’re are making a choice. And your choice equals no resolutions at all and no future friendship. A one sided dialogue is not a dialogue. Just FYI.

I’m glad you’re not sad or minimally sad. I’m glad you’re doing things and exploring SF. I wish you all the best, truly! While I remain steadfast in my protest to some of your behaviors towards me in the past in our relationship, I still can wish for you all the best.
Thanks,
Elizabeth


On Jul 28, 2018, at 6:17 PM, Elizabeth dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com<mailto:elidunc@hotmail.com>> wrote:
How are you otherwise?
Thanks,
Elizabeth

Date: July 28, 2018 at 5:17:54 PM PDT
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>
Subject: Re: Sigh
We tried it didn’t work. We tried so hard. We were truly so close. But there were things I couldn’t accept about you. I wanted you to change a part of you. We tried and were close. It’s not fair to you to expect you to change. I have faith that you’ll find the right person who will accept ALL of you as you are. I wasn’t willing to live with certain things. But it still hurts. And for me it may always hurt.
Thanks,
Elizabeth

I didn't have any affairs or relationships with anyone...in real life or online. You were my entire focus. You were my life. I constantly tried to make your life happier and better as much as I could.

Every day I cry. Every day I get mad at you all over. Every day I negotiate with myself. Every day I remind myself of why it didn’t work. Every day I struggle. Every day I keep as busy as possible. To forget. To move forward.
Thanks,
Elizabeth


On Jul 28, 2018, at 4:26 PM, Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
I cannot begin to explain how much I miss you. As I do things down here and explore the city, I am constantly thinking about you and how much I wish you were by my side...where you belong.

How did we screw things up so badly?

How did we let things get in the way?

I am so sad when I let my mind think about what we let slip away.

---Steve

Hi Matt…

Thanks for the quick conversation.

Here is my resume. Please note that I have had success at every stop, no matter the technology. Also, as mentioned, I have a technical background with the certifications to back it up. What I didn’t mention is that I am also extremely proficient at public speaking. I have spoken at conferences around the world to both technical and CxO audiences…some conferences were as large as 13k attendees. So, not only can I be my own SE, I can also be my own keynote speaker.  😊

Finally, I am one of those rare triple threats. I can speak business to the partner’s executive team, I can talk money to the sales and marketing teams, and then I can geek out with the technical guys in the backroom.

Let’s make this happen!!!


---Steve

body:
Hi Everyone...

I am writing with bad news.

The new executive team at Kroll has decided that having a channel is not the direction they would like to go , so as of this morning the team was let go. There is no more channel at Kroll.

Thanks for giving us a chance and for being so great to work with.

I am sure you will be hearing from me soon in some sort of channel role with another vendor.

Until then, I wish you the very best of luck.

Thanks.

---Steve


Note - Have y'all even considered expanding into Mexico? I know a guy that could help start up the Mexico business. 🙂


Steve Winfield
425-753-8897
swinfield@hotmail.com
subject: Kroll is killing off the channel
