From: Elizabeth Dunc <elidunc@hotmail.com>
Date: Friday, August 10, 2018 at 10:47 AM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: With Make America Dinner Again, politics gets a place at the table | The Seattle Times

This is my cousin who is getting married tomorrow here in Seattle . (Emily Nelson.)

https://www.seattletimes.com/life/food-drink/with-make-america-dinner-again-politics-gets-a-place-at-the-table/?utm_source=marketingcloud&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=Morning+Brief+8-8-18_8_8_2018&utm_term=
With Make America Dinner Again, politics gets a place at the table
Bethany Jean Clement<file:////author/cap-bethany-jean-clement/>Updated August 7, 2018 at 1:42 pm
[Image removed by sender. Emily Nelson, the host of the Seattle edition of Make America Dinner Again, chats with attendees over appetizers at the Commons on Queen Anne. (Rebekah Welch / The Seattle Times)]

In a divisive time, a nationwide organization is bringing people of divergent views together over dinner — with intense, cathartic results.

Ray is tall and lanky. He seems maybe a little shy, but sweet. Did he bring the macaroni and cheese? No, he brought some store-bought subway sandwiches. “Triple meat!” he says, with satisfaction. I brought couscous<https://www.seattletimes.com/life/food-drink/how-to-hack-amazon-gos-meal-kit-charred-corn-pearl-couscous/> made with veggie stock, in case of hungry vegetarians at the potluck, but I tell him about my dear departed grandmother’s small cattle ranch in Eastern Washington, and we discuss our mutual love of meat. “Beef’s my favorite,” he says. We are in full agreement about the greatness of steak.

Ray has come to Make America Dinner Again <http://www.makeamericadinneragain.com/> (M.A.D.A.)— a nationwide organization bringing people of divergent political views together for supper and conversation — after seeing a video on YouTube. (He didn’t want his last name used.) “I thought, ‘This is fantastic, that people are willing to talk,’ ” he says. He’s had people delete him on Facebook because of his politics. “Family and friends,” he says unhappily. “I haven’t defriended anyone,” he notes. His sincerity — his hope for connection — seems clear. Who does he support? “President Trump,” he pronounces extra clearly, seeming a little surprised to be saying it, to a stranger, in person and out loud.

It’s great that Ray’s here — Seattle-area M.A.D.A. chapter head Emily Nelson initially didn’t get a lot of potential dinner guests from the conservative side of the political spectrum. She reached out to a few Republican Facebook groups to get the party started; she’s hosted one other M.A.D.A. before, and this is the fourth in the area. The process to sign up is simple: Just go to makeamericadinneragain.com<http://www.makeamericadinneragain.com/> and answer a few questions, e.g., “Which of the following political perspectives do you identify with the most? Check all that apply :)” You can also find out how to be a host.

M.A.D.A. dinners are potluck, but you don’t have to bring anything; they’re free, though donations are encouraged. This one is held in Queen Anne’s All Saints Church event space, The Commons, a pretty room that’s not too big and not too small, with a couple of handsome wood beams overhead decked out with twinkly lights. The table is elegant, white-clothed with gold charger plates under china ones; the jazz soundtrack includes Nina Simone. The spread is formidable, enough food for a big party, but this is to be more intimate, just dinner for 10. As the guests arrive and make first-name tags, a wine cork pops. “That’s a good sound!” someone says.

Mark Fosdal, a friendly cancer researcher who lives on the Eastside and describes himself as “more to the right,” is the one who brought the macaroni, made with garlic, lots of butter, hot sauce, cheddar, mozzarella and Parmesan. Andrea Baker, who lives in Issaquah, emanates warmth as she talks about her busy life — she’s a singer/songwriter, mom, and church music director, and she and her husband also have a meat-and-cheese distribution business. She’s brought a very generous cheese board including aged Gouda, sweet sopressata and truffle pecorino.

Zawadi Morrow, also a musician and an adjunct professor of music at Seattle Pacific University who lives in Mountlake Terrace, is tossing a kale slaw, although he didn’t bring it. He’s too selfless to mention that he personally funded a lot of the organizer-prepared extra food — tri-tip and Hawaiian chicken kebobs, the slaw, more. Brendan Perko, the pastor of All Saints, brought chips and salsa and guacamole to represent his upbringing in Pueblo, Colorado, even though, he laughs, “I look like this” — tall and blonde and blue-eyed.

Emily calls us all to the table. An icebreaker game about similarities and differences reveals some funny ones: Ray and Zawadi differ on meat-matters, but are both part Irish and share a distaste for gin. Politics are one thing, Mark says, but at gin-haters, he draws the line — “I’m out of here!” Everybody laughs. Pastor Brendan is paired with Jessica Foster for an animated, obviously fun conversation; they both have earrings (“Two!” Brendan points out) and tattoos, but she’s not at all religious. Mark just did the Seattle-to-Portland bike ride; Andrea, he’s learned, isn’t that into the outdoors, as he tactfully puts it. “You can just say I don’t like it and I’m lazy, that’s fine!” she laughs.

Those who choose to dine with strangers with contentious topics on the table may be of a special creed; there’s a charge in the air, but the atmosphere already feels convivial. Emily builds a philosophical framework — “I believe that humans are amazing, and we can do better” — and lays down ground rules, explaining active listening, encouraging “I” statements and questions. We are instructed to expect, and allow, uncomfortable silences. Our whole selves, Emily says, are welcome at this table; she’s a calming presence, a preternaturally good host with a sense of humor too. Our safe word, she says, is “avocado.”

There are, it turns out, only a few moments of quiet, and they feel less awkward than thoughtful, portentous. “More wine helps!” Mark points out, and by the evening’s end, several bottles of Two-Buck Chuck will be gone through. The first question for the group is about feeling misunderstood vis-a-vis political leanings. Mark feels that with our present-day labels, so many assumptions get made — that if you describe yourself one way, you must watch Fox News all the time. “I just keep thinking of the word ‘assumption,’ ” Andrea agrees — as a person of faith, nonbelievers tend to categorize her one way, while those of other religions have their preconceptions, too.

“I feel misunderstood on almost every political point,” Ray says. “It’s really hard to just have a conversation … so many people have deleted me.” A respectful silence. Jessica talks about the notion of “triggers,” and how it’s become a shorthand buzzword for liberal-snowflake syndrome, but that for her, what it signifies is real. “Politics can be very personal,” she says.

As the evening goes on, it’s hard to believe how personal the conversation gets. A new question: What factor has influenced your politics the most? People talk about their upbringings, about religion or the lack thereof, about abusive parents, bigoted relatives, divorce and trauma and more. It’s almost shocking to hear such candid, entirely individual stories from real people, face to face.

Andrea says that “Since Ferguson, things feel really different for me.” As someone who’s upper-middle class, white and religious, she says, she’s realized she has to “Listen. Listen. Listen.”

Mark has taken to describing his politics more proactively, talking about how he does want to take care of the environment and of people. “Politics is me making a difference here at this dinner table, not yelling at the TV,” he says. Lauren Pattie discusses how our current polarized climate in some ways mirrors her past, in which she was exposed to both sides of the political debate, “caught between these two worlds.” It taught her how to talk about it, to listen carefully but still question: “Yes, but have you thought — perhaps we should consider …” she laughs. Brendan talks about the dual influences of being raised evangelical and finding Biggie Smalls. “I think I landed somewhere in the middle!” More laughter.

Fact-checking the president: Is it fair? Brendan starts to answer Ray’s question, then pauses. “Is this OK?” he asks Emily. “Can this conversation go here?” Absolutely, she says, and a civil but serious debate ensues. “Does he lie on purpose? Yes … Is he under a microscope more than others? Yes. Is that fair? Yes,” Brendan says. Mark asks photographer Rebekah Welch and I to explain journalism and bias, which we try our best to do succinctly, without bias. Later, he’ll give an impassioned explanation of why he thinks tolerance as a goal has been a terrible misstep — only putting up with each other isn’t working. We need, he says, actual engagement.

More important questions, more earnest talking and listening, then Emily asks everyone to say at once what their favorite dish has been. “Avocado!” one person shouts, and everybody laughs.

If you want to liven up your dinner party, try what Emily calls a “spectrum exercise.” Get everyone up from the table (“Bring your wine!”); designate one wall “strongly agree” and the garbage can “strongly disagree”; then ask a series of increasingly controversial political questions. What stands out here is less Ray ending up alone on one side, though it happens, or the women clumped by the garbage and the men spread out elsewhere, though that happens too. What stands out is everyone looking at where everyone else stands, thinking and talking about it in terms of the group, with openness and a feeling of belonging, no matter where they end up.

Zawadi takes off his shoes; at one point, he sits down on a couch that happens to be perfectly, comfortably positioned along the spectrum for him, to the group’s amusement. A question about abortion finds Brendan leaning against one wall, his face in his hands. Seeing the difficulty he has in taking his stance — human, conflicted, real — brings the room to silence.

Gun control. Immigration. Black Lives Matter. The electoral college. At one point, Brendan mimes pulling the pin out of a grenade and tossing it on the table, which is now messy with pens and notepads, crumpled-up napkins and half-full glasses. All the things you’re not supposed to bring up at a dinner party are raised, and it’s not a disaster. In fact, it’s nuanced and challenging, fun at times, intense at others, cathartic and comforting.

Toward the end, Lauren says that she’s a quarter Armenian, that her grandmother came here from Syria. “Thank god America was accepting refugees from Syria when my grandma needed somewhere to go,” she says. There’s always a new group vilified by the dominant group, she says; now it may be Muslims, but at one point, it was the Irish and Italians.

Eloquently, calmly, she discusses how conservatives call for bigger walls, while liberals call for bigger doors, when what we need is a bigger table — not to just keep people out or let them in, but to know and nourish each other. Listening and nodding all around.

Thanks,
Elizabeth

body:
The ability to understand what’s happening in the moment is more valuable than ever. And the ability to make confident decisions based on that understanding is essential for helping your organization adapt and thrive—even as circumstances change rapidly. Strengthen your organization’s resilience with an analytics service that helps you make informed decisions quickly, share insights across your organization, and generate actionable predictions.

Using the power of Microsoft Azure and Power BI, we can help you…

  *   Unify data silos and build a single end-to-end analytics platform to support all your business activities.
  *   Discover actionable insights from all of your data quickly and cost-effectively.
  *   Apply intelligent analytics to your data and go from hypothesis to validation in minutes.
  *   Generate reports that make it easier for more people at your organization to understand and work directly with data.

To get help planning and implementing your analytics program with Azure or get more information on what an analytics program can do for you, click on this link to set up a meeting:

Thanks.

SIG LINE
subject: Next for Power BI

Subject: GAME WEEK 1

Hello JV Parents,

 Great first scrimmage, it was great to get some live action, loved the effort and execution from the boys, with still a lot of room to grow. For week 1, We're looking for a volunteer to film the game, it's extremely crucial for us to have film to review with the boys to aid in our progression. Let me know if anyone can, again it would be a HUGE help for us.

Thank you all for the support, love this group!!

Best,

Coach Millard

subject: Fwd: Confirmation Letter - KJAPOT 09/08/18 - from Alaska Airlines

body:
Hi Teri...

Would you happen to have any availability tomorrow (Wednesday) or Thursday?

We are having a serious issue we need assistance with.

---Steve

Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
subject: Availability?

body:
Hi Danielle...

I am all set up in Rent Cafe, but thoroughly confused by the amounts shown in my two accounts (O2-511 and 05-301).

Can you take a look and tell me if I currently owe money?

—- Steve

Are you having any problems?

body:
Hi…

I thought I would add a few more items to help make my case.

Attached you will find my offer letter to verify income and a chart from Credit Karma showing the fall of my credit score in the last few months.

I am also adding some pictures so you can picture us living in the house.  ☺

[Gaby and Steve. Note the "Coming Soon" concept.  :-)]
Gaby and Steve letting everyone know they are moving to LV.

[Our kids.]
The two in the front are both off at college.
The two in the back still live with us.


Pictures so you can

From: Steve Winfield
Sent: Tuesday, July 16, 2019 2:22 PM
To: severinac@bhhsnv.com
Subject: 912 Cirrus Cloud


Hello…



My wife and I are interested in the home on 912 Cirrus Cloud; I realize you will want an official application at some point, but I wanted to provide a little background about us and our situation.



Gaby was born in Mexico, but came to the US in 2002 speaking only Spanish. Over the years, she has been a tireless worker, often working 2 or 3 jobs at a time, while still being the only real caregiver her kids ever knew. During this time, she taught herself English and tried to work on her education at every opportunity, knowing that both were needed to be successful in the US and to provide the best possible life for her children. She has worked her way from house cleaning, to food service, to dental assistant, to now being the back office manager of a very successful multi-doctor dental practice in Dublin, CA. With the move to Nevada, Gaby hopes to spend more time with the two boys still at home, continue to work on her English, and hopefully begin to take some college classes.



As for me, I am a native Texan, but hope you don’t hold that against me (☺).  After starting my professional career as a teacher and principal at the junior high level back in Texas, I transitioned to the technology world. As noted by my LinkedIn profile (https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevewinfieldtx/), I have been very successful in technology with many awards from my employers and accolades from my co-workers, but still always have found time to give back to the community. I have coached youth soccer, umpired little league baseball, volunteered at the schools, helped with Girl Scouts, worked backstage at a local community dance company, and even served time on the board of a non-profit organization helping support arts in the schools.



This last year, however, has not been the greatest. My move to the ridiculously expensive San Francisco area for a new job, paying the complete cost of two kids in college (their mother was unable to contribute this last year) and both my parents learning they have very serious medical issues requiring my assistance, combined with my new company vastly underperforming, resulted in me having to make some tough financial choices in the February/March time frame. I had to decide to either continue to provide for my family or to pay some of the credit card bills. I chose the former and decided to start looking for a new position that would allow me to move out of state to a less expensive living situation. So, my credit score went from low 700s in January/February, down to where it is now; but it won’t stay there long.



My new job is why we are moving to Nevada. In addition to no longer having to pay state income tax (approximately $600/month), train fare for the commute downtown (approximately $300/month), the drastic reduction in rental costs ($1200/month savings just for Steve’s apartment), and a pay raise (approximately $700/month after taxes), we will very quickly be able to begin to cover the missing payments and regain a decent credit score.



To help make us better candidates, we are willing to pay an additional deposit as well as possibly signing an 18-24 month lease.



Please give us a call to discuss and so we can further make our point.



Thanks.



---Steve



-------------------------------------------------------



Steve Winfield - Credit Score 470ish (was 700+ in February of this year)


·       Employer
o   Trustifi - Starting July 22
·       Channel Manager/Technical Sales
·       Income: $110k base plus $80k targeted commission

From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Date: Monday, May 21, 2018 at 11:59 AM
To: Carrie Roberts <croberts@mimecast.com>
Subject: Move to SF

Hi. I had a nice call with Kristen this morning.

I told her I could move down to SF within the month if y'all could provide any assistance with getting out of my apartment lease and renting a U-Haul.

However, as I think about it more, if y'all hired me this week, I wouldn't need any assistance at all and could still move within the next month.

Feel free to call me to discuss.

---Steve

Rate Details & Cancellation Policy
•

Rate Guarantee Limitation(s)

•
Changes in taxes or fees implemented after booking will affect the total room price.

Additional Information

•
Upon check-in an authorization request will be placed on your credit/debit card in an amount equal to the cost of the room, tax and incidental charges for the length of your stay (up to seven nights). If your stay exceeds seven nights, an additional authorization may be requested for the entire amount of your stay (room, tax and incidentals). Upon check-out, your payment card will be charged for the actual amount incurred during your stay.

Additional Redemption Information

•
Using your points will cover your room charge and tax. Additional service charges, additional bed fees (such as crib, pack and play, or rollaway bed), or resort fees may apply where applicable.

Hello Steve,

Thank you for your message and sharing your interest. I am curious if you are interested in assisting with the U8 age group as we have a need there. Or are you only interested in coaching your child?

Happy to hop on a call sometime this week to discuss.

Thank you,

On Mon, Jun 26, 2023 at 8:09 PM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
Hi. I am interested in being an assistant coach. I have almost 30 years of coaching experience in Texas, Washington, and last year with BUSC. I have coached YMCA, recreational, select, club, and high school. I am happy to jump on a call to discuss my philosophies of soccer and off coaching if you are interested.

Thanks.

—-Steve

From: Ballistic United Soccer Club <mailer@leagueapps.com<mailto:mailer@leagueapps.com>>
Sent: Monday, June 12, 2023 5:04:02 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com> <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>
Subject: Welcome BUSC Competitive Members!

[https://svite-league-apps-content.s3.amazonaws.com/7499/8981/83ea68ba-3297-4e9e-b9af-c8363fa2448a.jpeg]

________________________________

BUSC Competitive Player Members,

If you are receiving this email, your son recently attended our team placements and accepted an invite to join one of our competitive teams for the coming soccer season. We wanted to provide you a quick update on a few items and some of the timing of the next steps that will happen during the summer time!

As we enter the team placements we anticipate certain teams forming based upon our club's recent history but you never really know which teams will form until the placements are completed and invite offers are accepted or not. This year we are excited that a few teams formed which were not anticipated, so we are currently recruiting and interviewing coaching candidates for these teams.

Your team coaches will be reaching out to everyone soon, if they have not already, to introduce themselves and also remind everyone about some key dates and things that will take place during the summer. Some key upcoming items are listed below:

New players to the club will need to order uniforms quickly so they are ready for the upcoming season. Remember that this is a "fill-in" year, as it is the second year we are using the same uniforms as the previous season. A club representative will be in contact with you very soon and let you know how this process is handled.

All teams will need a parent to step up and become a team manager. Many teams might have a returning manager from the prior year but others may not so please begin to think if you would like to help your team and the club in this capacity.

The club will be conducting several competitive skills camps this summer starting two weeks from now on June 24th and running through July 21st! One of these camps will be run by professional coaches from Spain and will be an optional Pre Season Training Camp (See "UEFA PRO Ballistic Pre-Season Camp) below to register! Details on these camps can be found by clicking on these links.

Our return to club training is scheduled for the week of July 24th. Please do NOT worry if your family will be out of town on vacation as that is what the summer break is for! Just communicate that to your coach as needed.

As part of your Premier and Elite club fees, the club pays for the fall and spring league registration fees, any winter league fees, and 3 tournaments as well as the State Cup event for your son's team.

As part of your Select club fees, the club pays for the fall league registration fees, and 2 tournaments as well as the State Cup event for your son's team.

Your coaches and our technical staff will determine the tournaments we will attend. If a team chooses to attend additional tournaments they will need to be approved by the club TD/DOC and additional money will need to be collected from each family to pay for the additional event. The additional fees will be used to cover the costs of the additional tournaments and costs associated with the coaches travel (if needed).

As many of you are aware, northern California has lost about 40% of the licensed referees due to covid and the experience many referees are experiencing with poor sideline behavior. Our club does a fairly good job with ongoing education regarding this topic. We formed a new referee association (EBRA) about two years ago in partnership with Rage, San Ramon and Livermore Fusion. If anyone, including parents and players 13 & up, would like to consider becoming a licensed referee EBRA will be holding courses to become licensed. Those details can be found on the club website.

Finally, we are looking to create a "Club return to training celebration day" on a Sunday in late July or early August. We are in talks with the city about getting access to the Stanford Turf facility off Bernal ave for this event.This event will be a fun club culture and team building event with fun games and a barbecue for those who are interested! More details will be forthcoming!

The best way to get a hold of club leadership is through email and those emails can be found on the club website. Please reach out if you have any questions that your coach or team manager can not answer. We will be in touch every 2-3 weeks during the summer break to provide club updates that are not addressed in our club monthly newsletter.

Enjoy your summer! it goes by fast!

Hi Calum…

I completed the online application for the VP role as Keith talked with me about both the Channel VP as well as the AMD Alliance role.

He also mentioned that he wanted me to have two more interviews in the next couple days (US Sales lead and a sales rep, I believe).

To make things faster to schedule, I can make myself available at any of the following times:


  *   Tuesday: 6am PST to 11:30am PST and 1pm PST to 8pm PST
  *   Wednesday: 6am PST to 7am PST and 9am to 8pm PST
  *   Thursday: 6am PST to 8pm PST

I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Thanks.

---Steve

swinfield@hotmail.com

And I wanted to provide some advance notice that you will start to receive some additional emails requesting new hire information or forms to be completed & submitted.  As well as our policies for review and sign off (those via docusign).

The previous email with attachments that I just sent (see below) to you requires your immediate attention, to complete forms, submit supporting documents and to return to me as soon as possible.  So I may initiate all the setups before your first day.

I will also need to be informed if you will use your own mobile phone and select a stipend (see offer letter for more details) or if you wish to have a company phone assigned to you.  And we issue company laptops.  I will need to know your selection of either windows or apple.

If you have any questions regarding and of the email or policies please feel free to contact me directly.

Thank you for your assistance.

Carol Knox
HR/Payroll Generalist
C: 650-245-0784 | www.cancom.com<http://www.cancom.com/>

[cid:image001.png@01D6FBEF.234C3240]


From: Carol Knox
Sent: Friday, February 5, 2021 2:27 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Steve Winfield * Welcome to CANCOM!
Importance: High
Sensitivity: Confidential

Hi Steve,

Welcome to CANCOM!  We are pleased to have you joining our team soon.

CANCOM is a full-service, award-winning, global IT Solution Provider and Systems Integrator focused on delivering complex and custom IT solutions to our clients. Established in 1994, we have greater than 20 years of experience as an Systems Integrator and VAR. Our value is simple; we offer pragmatic, vendor neutral, solutions to help our clients manage risk, scale, grow and control their technology infrastructure. Our diverse team of engineers and architects are certified and accredited experts in hands-on delivery and management for all of the solutions we bring to market. We focus on remaining objective, informed and place our core emphasis around operational impact, execution and end results. CANCOM has been listed on the CRN Tech Elite 250 consecutively for the past six years, a recognition awarded to the top 250 IT Solution Providers with the deepest and broadest set of certifications from key technology vendors.

In preparation for your first day, please review and complete all forms prior to your start date and return to my attention as soon as possible:


  1.  Items to return prior to your start date:
     *   Employee Data Sheet:  Please complete and return to me as soon as possible so I may begin your ADP setup and access.
     *   Background Authorization Form:  Please complete the attachments and return to me as soon as possible.
     *   New Employee Introduction: We like to send out a company-wide email introducing our new employees. Please let me know if you would like me to include any hobbies, information about your family, etc. Do you travel? Play a sport? Sing in a band? Newly married? Whatever you would like to share. However, if you aren’t big on sharing, please let me know and your manager will be happy to keep it strictly professional.
     *   Photo: We would like a close up photo of you for your email profile.  This is not visible to anyone outside the company.



  1.  Items to bring with you on your first day:
     *   USCIS Employment Eligibility Verification (Better known as the I-9): On page 3, you will find a list of acceptable documents which establish identity and authorization to work in the US. Please bring the original of either one item from list A OR one from list B AND one from list C with you on your first day. If you have any concerns about acceptable documentation, please give me a call at the number below.
     *   Direct Deposit: Should you wish to sign up for direct deposit, please bring a voided check with you.

I will schedule our New Hire Orientation meeting within your first three days of employment.

In the meantime, should you have any questions, please feel free to email me or give me a call.

Thank you.
Carol Knox
HR/Payroll Generalist
C: 650-245-0784 | www.cancom.com<http://www.cancom.com/>

body:
Hi Sabrina ...

I just learned that I won $500 in free play at the event yesterday and I have to pick it up by 3pm this afternoon.

That's a problem since we left Tahoe about an hour ago.

If i had known the announcement about who won wouldn't happen until 11:30am today then we would have waited. However, since it was announced at the event yesterday that the winners would have credit put directly onto their cards... and I didn't have anything put onto my card when I last checked this morning around 8am, I assumed I had not won.

I called the events center to ask what to do and they said it would be up to you to decide if I could get an exception to the 3pm deadline to pick up my winnings.

What do you need from me to make it happen?
subject: I just learned I won?!?!

body:
Hi. I don't see where you list the hourly rate or the number of movers.

Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
________________________________
From: Spartan Moving Systems Inc. <ashish@spartanmoving.net>
Sent: Tuesday, October 2, 2018 8:03:48 AM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com
Subject: F.A.Q

[http://eagle.hellomoving.com/SPARTANMO/IMAGES/logo.gif]
Tuesday, 10/02/2018

Dear Steve W.,


What are the charges for a local move?
Local moves are hourly charge based. Once the completion time exceeds our minimum charge we charge prorated rate for every 15 minutes. There is a charge for DOUBLE DRIVE TIME between the first location and the last location. We include free use of moving blankets, tape and Wardrobe boxes. All other packing supplies are charged as used (Mattress Covers, Boxes, Wrapping Paper, Shrink Wrap, etc’).

What is Double Driving Time?
Double drive time actually saves you money and also protects you from unscrupulous movers that might charge “portal to portal”. In essence double drive time means you don’t get charged f or the time it takes to get to your home from our warehouse, or in other words the drive to your origin.
We also don’t charge you for the time it takes after the move is complete to get back to our warehouse.
Instead, the time to get from your origin (starting point – where you are moving from) to your destination (ending point – where you are moving to) is logged, and then doubled. This is supposed to represent all of the drive time involved in your move.
If you think about it it makes sense. The reason the PUC<http://www.cpuc.ca.gov/PUC/transportation/> does it that way is because you can actually “see” the amount of time it takes to get from your origin to your destination. You would not be able to know how long it takes to get to your origin from the warehouse, or to get to the warehouse from your destination so movers aren’t allowed to use this. So instead , the law says to pay twice for the time in the “middle” to represent the total drive time involved with your move. You can actually read or download the entire Tariff on our site by clicking here<http://www.cpuc.ca.gov/NR/rdonlyres/F3BD9608-40B3-425D-A598-300546203164/0/MaximumRateTariff4Rev011212.pdf>. You’ll find information regarding the double diving time on page 9.

Do offer full packnig service?
Full packing service is available for ALL INCLUSIVE hourly rate including all packing supplies and wrapping materials.

What is the proper way to pack my belongings?
If you decide to pack yourself pick a room in your house or apartment to be your center of packing operations. This makes the process more efficient and limits much of the mess to one room, which will help lessen the stress of moving. Choose a room with a large table surface so you can wrap while standing up. Leave yourself ample time.
There is a science to packing moving boxes. It’s key get specialized boxes for fragile or uniquely shaped items. You can purchase these boxes from moving or storage companies. You may have to pay a little extra, but it will be worth it when your items arrive undamaged.

  *   Don’t pack your books, or other heavy items, in large boxes. It sounds convenient, but you will NEVER be able to move it. And these boxes often fall apart under the pressure of too much weight, thus damaging your goods.

  *   Pack heavier items at the bottom of boxes and avoid packing heavy items with breakable items.

  *   Pack unused items first. Start with the garage, spare bedrooms, backs of closets, or cedar chests.

  *   Make a donation pile as you begin sifting through your items. Keep them in one spot and call ahead for donation pick-up for a few days before your move date. This is tax deductible and will decrease your shipment, which will lessen your moving cost.

  *   Wall art and mirrors are better wrapped with blankets or towels and protected in long, flat boxes.

  *   For breakable kitchen items consider purchasing “dish boxes” to hold your dishes in place. They’re more durable than most boxes.

  *   Expect to use several more boxes than you estimate, have several “extras” on hand in case you run out.

  *   Electronics: Electronics are usually important items to the family, so pack them carefully. If you have the original boxes, use these. Wrap the item with a blanket, towel, bubble wrap before you slide it into a box. Fill in any empty space with packing paper. If you can’t find the original box purchase one from a moving company. It’s worth the additional expense. Or have your moving company pack these items for you. Most moving companies will offer this service. They start by removing the electronic item’s cords, wrap them, label each appropriately, and then pack them in a bag that is packed inside the box. Use a blanket, towel, or moving pad to wrap the item, then slide it into the box. If there’s still space left over, use crumpled-up packing paper or rolled-up socks to fill in the holes.

Can I leave items inside the dressers?
We recommend to remove all the contents from the furniture for safety reasons.

Do you move plants?
We do not move plants. An exception can me made based on the distance between the 2 locations and the size of the plants. Fragile pots have to be packed properly inside boxes in order to be moved safely.

Do you move appliances?
Our movers are all professionals but not certified technicians. We DO NOT take any liability when disconnecting the appliances is involved with water or gas connections. You will have to be present when we disconnect the appliances for liability reasons.

Can you dispose used packing supplies?
We can dispose used packing supplies, there is a disposal fee of $75.00.

Do you offer other hauling/disposal services?
We offer disposal services. The charge is by volume of space. Our rate is $150.00 per 100 CF with a minimum charge of 200 CF.

When do I need to get a parking permit and how do I get it?
Parking permits are a lot more expensive than parking citation tickets. We recommend to get a parking permit only if we risk other traffic or block the entire road/bus lane. In case we get a parking citation for double parking we add it to the final bill. Parking permits are available through www.movingpermits.com<http://www.movingpermits.com/> and www.sfmta.com


What are the payment methods?
We accept all major credit cards (VIsa, Amex, MC.), cash and money orders. Deposit might be required when moving during the end of the month and weekends.

Can you provide a certificate of General Liability Insurance?
We have GL coverage up to $2,000,000. 00. Proof of insurance can be provided for no additional charge upon request.
Adding your location/HOA/Property management as additional insured is involved with a fee of $75.00

Please feel free to contact me if you have any more questions or concerns regarding your move.



Sincerely,

Ashish Sharma
Moving Consultant
Spartan Moving Systems Inc.
877-417-6060
Fax: 877-417-6060
ashish@spartanmoving.net
http://www.spartanmoving.com
US DOT: 1783494  MC: 650181
Mover Registration: 0190543

If you no longer wish to receive emails from Spartan Moving Systems Inc., please click unsubscribe<http://eagle.hellomoving.com/wc.dll?mputil~removeemail~&CUSTID=2862&EMAILTO=swinfield@hotmail.com&JOBID=62834398>

From: Travis Boak <boaktravis6@gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, January 8, 2026 7:17 AM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: EXCITING OPPORTUNITIES AT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, CROWDSTRIKE, AND ZSCALER

Alright Steve,
we’re ready to proceed you forward. Before I lock your profile into the internal slate, I need to align on a few non-negotiables to ensure we secure the best possible conditions and positions for you.

Please confirm the following in one message:
1) Work format preference: Remote / Hybrid / On-site
2) Compensation range you intend to command based on the value you bring
3) Existing Resume

Once aligned, I’ll share the job description for your review — this is an internal, high-priority mandate, and access is restricted to shortlisted talent only.

Reply directly with the above questions. Once we have clarity, we move fast.

Subject: EXCITING OPPORTUNITIES AT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, CROWDSTRIKE, AND ZSCALER


Steve,

You have been selected for a high-priority shortlist following a comprehensive audit of the world’s most effective cybersecurity channel leaders. My firm has been retained to handpick the next phase of global alliance leadership for an elite tier of organizations including Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, Cisco, and Microsoft. Your career trajectory—spanning foundational leadership at Microsoft to your current role as Global VP at WireX Systems—establishes you as a benchmark for the caliber of executive we are targeting.

Your sophisticated mastery of "Partner Program Construction" and your deep expertise in cyber risk channels at Kroll and Cybereason make you a critical asset for the VP of Global Cloud Alliances role at Palo Alto Networks. The hiring manager is looking for your specific ability to architect complex partner portal infrastructures and execute go-to-market strategies that bridge the gap between legacy distribution and modern cloud security. This is an internal, high-priority position sourced strictly through direct headhunting; it is not a public listing and is being filled with a focus on immediate global scale.

This search is extremely time-sensitive. I am moving you to the final review stage and will be submitting my vetted recommendations to the hiring manager within the next 48 hours. Respond immediately to confirm your interest so we can align on the specifics and connect you with the team to discuss the application trajectory. If you are not interested, please let me know so I can release the slot. Once we align, you move forward fast.

On behalf of the team, we very much look forward to you joining us at Cybereason!

Malicious Life Podcast:

We would like to invite you to check out Malicious Life - our company podcast - whether you're new to or a veteran of the security industry. Its episodes contain everything you've ever wanted to know about the history of malware, security, and the roots of the industry we all work in.

body:
Yes.

________________________________
From: Travis Boak <boaktravis6@gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, January 8, 2026 7:25:49 AM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: EXCITING OPPORTUNITIES AT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, CROWDSTRIKE, AND ZSCALER

Alright Steve,
Kindly go through the job description and let me know if it align well with your professional goal
Company

Palo Alto Networks
(Global Cybersecurity Platform | Cloud, Network & AI Security)

Title

Vice President, Global Cloud Alliances & Strategic Partnerships
(Internal variants may include: VP, Global Alliances; VP, Cloud & Technology Partnerships)

Location

Santa Clara, CA (HQ)
Flexible for major tech hubs (Seattle, Austin, New York)
(Global remit)

Role Overview

Palo Alto Networks is seeking a senior global alliances executive to architect, scale, and lead its cloud and strategic partner ecosystem worldwide. This role owns the design and execution of partner programs across hyperscalers, technology partners, GSIs, MSSPs, and regional channel ecosystems.

The VP of Global Cloud Alliances will play a critical role in Palo Alto Networks’ cloud security growth strategy, bridging legacy channel distribution with modern cloud-native go-to-market motions while driving measurable revenue impact at global scale.

Key Responsibilities

  *   Define and execute global cloud alliances and strategic partner strategy

  *   Own relationships with hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and key technology partners

  *   Architect partner program structures, incentives, and enablement frameworks

  *   Drive joint GTM motions with GSIs, MSSPs, and cloud ecosystem partners

  *   Partner with Sales, Product, and Marketing to align partner strategy with revenue goals

  *   Build and scale global partner operations, portals, and enablement platforms

  *   Track and optimize partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue

  *   Recruit, lead, and develop senior global alliances leaders

Ideal Candidate Profile

  *   15+ years of experience in global alliances, channels, or partner leadership within cybersecurity or enterprise software

  *   Deep expertise in cloud security, cyber risk, and enterprise channel ecosystems

  *   Proven experience building and scaling global partner programs at enterprise scale

  *   Strong understanding of both legacy distribution and cloud-native GTM models

  *   Executive presence with ability to influence C-suite partners and internal leadership

Compensation (Executive Level)

Base Salary:
$270,000 – $350,000

Annual Bonus:
40% – 60% of base

Equity (RSUs):
$400,000 – $900,000+ (multi-year vesting)

Total Annual Compensation (All-In):
$700,000 – $1.4M+

Why This Role Is Critical

  *   Ownership of Palo Alto Networks’ most strategic global alliances

  *   Direct influence on cloud security revenue growth and partner-driven scale

  *   High visibility with executive leadership and hyperscaler partners

  *   Clear path to SVP-level leadership within a top-tier cybersecurity platform


On Thu, Jan 8, 2026 at 2:19 PM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:

  1.  Prefer remote, but am open to whatever is needed.
  2.  150k-180k base and a 60:40 split.
  3.  Attached



From: Travis Boak <boaktravis6@gmail.com<mailto:boaktravis6@gmail.com>>
Sent: Thursday, January 8, 2026 7:17 AM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>
Subject: Re: EXCITING OPPORTUNITIES AT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, CROWDSTRIKE, AND ZSCALER



Alright Steve,

we’re ready to proceed you forward. Before I lock your profile into the internal slate, I need to align on a few non-negotiables to ensure we secure the best possible conditions and positions for you.



Please confirm the following in one message:

1) Work format preference: Remote / Hybrid / On-site

2) Compensation range you intend to command based on the value you bring

3) Existing Resume



Once aligned, I’ll share the job description for your review — this is an internal, high-priority mandate, and access is restricted to shortlisted talent only.



Reply directly with the above questions. Once we have clarity, we move fast.





On Thu, Jan 8, 2026 at 2:15 PM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:

Yes…I am interested.





From: Travis Boak <boaktravis6@gmail.com<mailto:boaktravis6@gmail.com>>
Sent: Thursday, January 8, 2026 6:57 AM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: EXCITING OPPORTUNITIES AT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, CROWDSTRIKE, AND ZSCALER



Steve,

You have been selected for a high-priority shortlist following a comprehensive audit of the world’s most effective cybersecurity channel leaders. My firm has been retained to handpick the next phase of global alliance leadership for an elite tier of organizations including Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, Cisco, and Microsoft. Your career trajectory—spanning foundational leadership at Microsoft to your current role as Global VP at WireX Systems—establishes you as a benchmark for the caliber of executive we are targeting.

Your sophisticated mastery of "Partner Program Construction" and your deep expertise in cyber risk channels at Kroll and Cybereason make you a critical asset for the VP of Global Cloud Alliances role at Palo Alto Networks. The hiring manager is looking for your specific ability to architect complex partner portal infrastructures and execute go-to-market strategies that bridge the gap between legacy distribution and modern cloud security. This is an internal, high-priority position sourced strictly through direct headhunting; it is not a public listing and is being filled with a focus on immediate global scale.

This search is extremely time-sensitive. I am moving you to the final review stage and will be submitting my vetted recommendations to the hiring manager within the next 48 hours. Respond immediately to confirm your interest so we can align on the specifics and connect you with the team to discuss the application trajectory. If you are not interested, please let me know so I can release the slot. Once we align, you move forward fast.



--



[Kind regards,]

[photo]

I did this on the train coming in today. Sorry for the length, but I had lots of thoughts.

———————

Goddess Key Holder

--> First, need consistent branding. Should be The Indigo Goddess. Second, while  the idea of being caged may be interesting to some guys, it is totally impractical for almost all men who have actual lives. So, for many, you become a non starter right off the bat because of the name. Heck, you and I might end up in a very serious relationship, and the thought of being caged makes me nervous. <—

Introduction
Worship the Tantric Goddess

--> Throwing Tantric in here implies sex more than domination. Maybe "Submit to the Indigo Goddess" would be better. <—

Stand in your erotic power. Even when you’re on your knees with your hands bound behind your back. Explore your darkness. Grow stronger. I am a lifestyle and Professional Domme and a Tantric lover of Women. I entertain the occasional boy by putting him in his proper place--kneeling at the feet of a Goddess I'm a compassionate Mistress guiding you with stern and loving discipline and rewarding you by allowing you to give me pleasure. I employ punishment, chastity and financial Domination as I see fit.

--> I am confused by this paragraph quite a bit. In the first four sentences you are talking about the guy growing stronger and embracing his darkness through submission? For a guy, we see submission as a cathartic stress relief, not a way to gain strength. Also, we don't see it has dark at all, unless you are talking about pretty deep and severe BDSM, which most guys you are trying to recruit are not into that level. <—

--> Then you get into saying you are a lover of women (which is cool), but you only rarely see a guy. It should be that you are a tantric lover of women, but love to dominate males and make even the strongest, most successful men submit to your will within a session. <—

--> You should add sensual domination to your list since that is what a lot of guys fantasize about. <—

--> Also, a lot of guys fantasize about "forced bi" scenarios. Have you ever explored that as part of your service? <—

My Ideal Person
I am seeking other Pro Dommes for professional collaboration as well as aspiring Dommes desiring mentorship with the goal of joining me in Domination sessions.

--> Your ideal person should be the older and successful male that appreciates the finer things in life and is appreciates genuine, thoughtful, and customized experiences. <—

--> Also, why even put an add for other Dommes on your profile? Are successful Dommes really reading the profiles of other Dommes in hopes of making business arrangements? If you really want to find some other Dommes to work with, we need to figure out why they would want to work with you and then actively find and recruit them. <—

Submissive playmates are required to provide generous financial tribute in addition to gifts of gratitude.

--> Change this to something like "It should be understood that there is a value for my experience doing what I do, so a tribute for my time and knowledge will be expected." When you say "generous", it will scare a number of guys off. <—

Highly qualified lifestyle slaves may inquire about the following service positions: \\\*Housemaid/sissy maid: 3-5 hours, 2-3X/week

--> Don't put the hours. <—

  \\\*Ghost writer for Femme Domme tales and instruction manual. Must possess excellent writing skills.

--> What!!! Am I being replaced already? 😜<—

 \\\*Personal social media curator for my professional promotion and client screening \\\*Dungeon + part-time living space provider and service slave. You provide the space. I’ll make it into our dungeon where we will entertain clients under my command. You will serve and collaborate

—> This goes away since we are most likely doing our thing. <—

\\*Photographer and/or fetish model (faces may be hidden)

—> we need to list out what the models will be expected to do. Some may be excited about foot worship while others bondage and other anal play. <—

 \** am currently seeking a special person to accompany me as my collared slave/pet for the SF Folsom Street Fair on September 30th. Privileges include removing my high-heel boots and massaging my feet, taking me shopping for artisan fetish wear, and kneeling at my feet while I pleasure other women. I enjoy all genders and orientations. I prefer mature individuals.

—> How long is the day? Maybe have this person do half of the day and the shopping, then let me do the last half of the day so you can relax and just have fun without feeling like you are working. <—

\\\* I receive more messages then I can respond to. If you’re interested in the activities or positions I mention in my profile, specify when you message me. Respectful, detailed messages are most likely to get my attention.

Thanks for the kind wishes…I hope the same for your family.

As a quick FYI, I will be sending you an email in January from my new job as SVP-Cyber Risk Channel with Kroll, Llc., to start the SI/VAR channel for the US.

Among a number of other risk mitigation services and solutions that might be interesting to your customer, Kroll offers a MDR service and a Digital Forensics/Incident Response solution that compliments O365 and various flavors of Microsoft Defender (as well as SentinelOne, Watchguard, Carbon Black, and others).

Olive & Goose could be one of the first channel partners in Kroll’s new worldwide program! 😊

I look forward to talking to you in the New Year; until then have a great holiday!!!

---Steve

body:
First off, it is the end of the season and I don't want to make any big deal out of this. I am not looking for anything to happen. I only wanted to make you aware of what happened so you didn't hear it through the grapevine.

If you still need to talk to people, the parents I know were there are:

Henry’s dad, Ken Yeung
Jeffrey's dad, Jim Lee
Ryan's dad, Siju Varghese
Jaspreet's dad, Inderpreet Sandhu
Zain’s mom, Natasha Malala

The two coaches on the other team and the referee were also witnesses to the constant coaching from the sideline.

The final interaction at halftime occurred in front of the entire team, which is why I told him it wasn't the time or place to have the discussion.

Call me if you'd like.

---Steve

425-753-8897


________________________________
From: Randy James <randyjames@busc.org>
Sent: Saturday, March 4, 2023 1:25:24 PM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Subject: Re: Ian and Ely Ford

Hey Steve, sorry to hear that that happened. Could you provide any names of some parents that were in attendance? The way I can also get some information from them as well. Thanks, Randy.

On Sat, Mar 4, 2023 at 9:43 AM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
Hi Randy...

I had an issue at today's game with Ian and Ely's dad. He was constantly coaching his kids from the parent's sideline.  So much so that his kids were not listening to my coaching because they were focusing on their dad's voice. I tried numerous times during the game to get his attention to get him to stop to no avail. At halftime, I walked across the field to talk to him. instead of talking to me, he turned and walked away so I had no choice but to call out to him saying that I needed his kids to listen to me and for him to not coach from the sideline. I returned to the team to give them some halftime adjustments when the dad came over to defend his coaching from the sideline. I told him that this was not the time or place for this discussion, but he continued. I told him again this was not the time or place. This time he got mad and said he wouldn't allow me to speak to him like that. He told his kids to grab their gear and they left before the second half started.

After the game, I asked the other parents if they heard me say anything inappropriate and they all agreed I did the right thing. Even the referee said he could verify that I said nothing rude and that the parent was wrong.

Anyway. I just thought you'd want to know what happened.

Happy to jump on a call if needed.

Thanks.

---Steve

425-753-8897
subject: Re: Ian and Ely Ford

ADVICE TO INTERNATIONAL PASSENGERS ON LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY

Passengers embarking upon a journey involving an ultimate destination or a stop in a country other than the country of departure are advised that the provisions of an international treaty (the Warsaw Convention, the 1999 Montreal Convention, or other treaty), as well as a carrier’s own contract of carriage or tariff provisions, may be applicable to their entire journey, including any portion entirely within the countries of departure and destination. The applicable treaty governs and may limit the liability of carriers to passengers for death or personal injury, destruction or loss of, or damage to, baggage, and for delay of passengers and baggage.

Additional protection can usually be obtained by purchasing insurance from a private company. Such insurance is not affected by any limitation of the carrier’s liability under an international treaty. For further information please consult your airline or insurance company representative.

Received. Thank you.

From: Kristin Lilley [Vassiliadis ES] <lillekm@nv.ccsd.net>
Sent: Wednesday, March 3, 2021 12:24 PM
To: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Cc: gaby05malibu42@gmail.com; Rachel Levandusky [Vassiliadis ES] <leeners@nv.ccsd.net>
Subject: Re: Andrew's WIDA Assessments

This is a reminder that Andrew is scheduled to take the Reading & Listening Tests on Monday, March 22nd from 9am-11am and the Speaking & Writing Tests on Wednesday, March 24th from 9am-11am. Please reply to this email letting me know you have received it so I can put Andrew on my list. If you have any questions, please let me know.

On Mon, Feb 22, 2021 at 6:25 PM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
Sounds good. Thank you.


________________________________
From: Kristin Lilley [Vassiliadis ES] <lillekm@nv.ccsd.net<mailto:lillekm@nv.ccsd.net>>
Sent: Monday, February 22, 2021 12:53:51 PM
To: swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com> <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>; gaby05malibu42@gmail.com<mailto:gaby05malibu42@gmail.com> <gaby05malibu42@gmail.com<mailto:gaby05malibu42@gmail.com>>
Cc: Rachel Levandusky [Vassiliadis ES] <leeners@nv.ccsd.net<mailto:leeners@nv.ccsd.net>>
Subject: Andrew's WIDA Assessments


Hello, my name is Kristin Lilley and I serve as the English Language Learner Testing Coordinator at Vassiliadis Elementary School. I am writing to you to schedule an appointment to assess Andrew's annual English proficiency to determine if he has shown growth in the English language. This is a federal requirement under the Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA) of 2015.



Since your child is still engaging in distance learning, we would need you to bring him to school for the assessments. The assessments will be taken at school over two days.



Andrew is scheduled to take the Reading & Listening Tests on Monday, March 22nd from 9am-11am and the Speaking & Writing Tests on Wednesday, March 24th from 9am-11am.



If these dates and times do not work for you, please email me back as soon as possible, so we can find a different time.



Students and teachers will be required to wear a mask through the duration of all assessments and will maintain social distancing at all times. Please use the attached Parent and Guardian Guidance: Checking Your Student Before Leaving Home<https://drive.google.com/file/d/15q7t7y4zU-gAO_rg1KylJRwRSgIz57O4/view?usp=sharing> prior to bringing your child to school. If you or your child are experiencing any COVID-19 symptoms prior to coming on campus for the assessment, please keep your child at home and contact me at (702) 799-1420 to reschedule the assessment.



If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me through email or phone at (702) 799-1420 ext 4115.

--
[https://docs.google.com/uc?export=download&id=184-GZ0XtYNdl9Oz3tZRtjtO8B3DJjFn5&revid=0B4SH-eGYxpYwWFp1NzVKMEVPbjlzOHZwSHI3QTRneXNaalhJPQ]




--
[https://docs.google.com/uc?export=download&id=184-GZ0XtYNdl9Oz3tZRtjtO8B3DJjFn5&revid=0B4SH-eGYxpYwWFp1NzVKMEVPbjlzOHZwSHI3QTRneXNaalhJPQ]

Do you know who y’all  talked to over there?

Ideally, I really want to be in that mid to upper $170k range.

Feel free to call me and discuss.

---Steve

Furthermore, without advance notice to the Passenger or purchaser, Southwest may cancel such reservations, or any other reservations that it believes, in its sole discretion, were made without intent to travel. body:
John and I texted some today about where we are and what we are doing. They are coming to San José in a couple months to visit a couple in the lifestyle. I said it would be great to see the two of them and meet their friends. Jonny asked if I wanted him to introduce us before they come. I said I was fine with it, but it was up to you.

The guy is a multi millionaire and has a beach house get away house in SF!!!

I think Christina it's going to text you and ask if she can give the rich guy your phone number.

This is a picture of the guy and his girlfriend and the view from the beach house.

[Image.jpeg][Image.jpeg]

body:
Hi All:

Here are my thoughts:


  1.  Let’s get him into 2nd semester of Algebra as soon as possible. He was doing really well with finishing the 1st semester, so I think he can work through the 2nd semester pretty quickly.
  2.  If we see that he is making good progress on the math, could we then sign him up for online 2nd semester US History in January or February?
  3.  Can we have a parent-teacher-student conference on Monday, December 10th in the afternoon to discuss the Biology situation?

Thanks.

---Steve

425-753-8897


From: Heather Cameron <hcameron@pleasantonusd.net>
Date: Tuesday, November 27, 2018 at 10:16 AM
To: Erin Moran <emoran@pleasantonusd.net>
Cc: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>, Joshua Butterfield <jbutterfield@pleasantonusd.net>, Patty Blair <pblair@pleasantonusd.net>
Subject: Re: Notice of Final Grade

Hi Everyone,
Erin, thanks for sharing the update that Luis finished his first semester of algebra I.  That's great news!  He now has to move on to the second semester of algebra I to complete his math requirement for graduation.  Luis still needs to complete the second semester of US History.  He will need to add US History to his schedule next semester and drop probably PE.  However, we could look at removing the college-prep status if it becomes a struggle.  Luis needs all his classes to graduate and I'm worried about his biology and economics classes since he's currently failing.  His biology teacher says he hasn't turned in any work and puts it little effort.  It's going to be hard to graduate from Foothill if he can't keep all his grades at D or higher.

Best regards,
Heather Cameron
Foothill High School
School Counselor (alpha Sh-Z)


On Fri, Nov 16, 2018 at 7:51 AM Erin Moran <emoran@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:emoran@pleasantonusd.net>> wrote:
I'm copying his counselor Mrs Cameron, as that will be coordinated through her and there may be other options that are available in spanish as I believe this program only has courses in English.  Typically, only seniors are able to continue taking these online courses during the school year, expect for special circumstances and since she oversees his overall progress she'll be able to give you more information.  Everyone is out of the office until Monday after thanksgiving so she will get back to you when we return.
Erin

On Fri, Nov 16, 2018 at 7:47 AM Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>> wrote:
Thanks for this Erin...

Now that the math is done, how do we get him set up for the semester of US History he needs to do? Is there a Spanish language version of it by chance?

---Steve

From: Erin Moran <emoran@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:emoran@pleasantonusd.net>>
Date: Friday, November 16, 2018 at 7:45 AM
To: "lt7839@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:lt7839@pleasantonusd.net>" <lt7839@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:lt7839@pleasantonusd.net>>, Gaby Sanchez <gaby_malibu42@yahoo.com<mailto:gaby_malibu42@yahoo.com>>, Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>>, Heather Cameron <hcameron@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:hcameron@pleasantonusd.net>>, Heather Pereira <hpereira@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:hpereira@pleasantonusd.net>>, Julie Lansbarkis <jlansbarkis@pleasantonusd.net<mailto:jlansbarkis@pleasantonusd.net>>

body:
Hello…



My wife and I are interested in the home on 754 Foreign Reef Way; I realize you will want an official application at some point, but I wanted to provide a little background about us and our situation.



Gaby was born in Mexico, but came to the US in 2002 speaking only Spanish. Over the years, she has been a tireless worker, often working 2 or 3 jobs at a time, while still being the only real caregiver her kids ever knew. During this time, she taught herself English and tried to work on her education at every opportunity, knowing that both were needed to be successful in the US and to provide the best possible life for her children. She has worked her way from house cleaning, to food service, to dental assistant, to now being the back office manager of a very successful multi-doctor dental practice in Dublin, CA. With the move to Nevada, Gaby hopes to spend more time with the two boys still at home, continue to work on her English, and hopefully begin to take some college classes.



As for me, I am a native Texan, but hope you don’t hold that against me (☺).  After starting my professional career as a teacher and principal at the junior high level back in Texas, I transitioned to the technology world. As noted by my LinkedIn profile (https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevewinfieldtx/), I have been very successful in technology with many awards from my employers and accolades from my co-workers, but still always have found time to give back to the community. I have coached youth soccer, umpired little league baseball, volunteered at the schools, helped with Girl Scouts, worked backstage at a local community dance company, and even served time on the board of a non-profit organization helping support arts in the schools.



This last year, however, has not been the greatest. My move to the ridiculously expensive San Francisco area for a new job, paying the complete cost of two kids in college (their mother was unable to contribute this last year) and both my parents learning they have very serious medical issues requiring my assistance, combined with my new company vastly underperforming, resulted in me having to make some tough financial choices in the February/March time frame. I had to decide to either continue to provide for my family or to pay some of the credit card bills. I chose the former and decided to start looking for a new position that would allow me to move out of state to a less expensive living situation. So, my credit score went from low 700s in January/February, down to where it is now; but it won’t stay there long.



My new job is why we are moving to Nevada. In addition to no longer having to pay state income tax (approximately $600/month), train fare for the commute downtown (approximately $300/month), the drastic reduction in rental costs ($1200/month savings just for Steve’s apartment), and a pay raise (approximately $700/month after taxes), we will very quickly be able to begin to cover the missing payments and regain a decent credit score.



To help make us better candidates, we are willing to pay an additional deposit as well as possibly signing an 18-24 month lease.



Also, I am attaching my offer letter to verify my base income and also a screenshot from Credit Karma showing the last year of my credit score. You can see it take a nose dive about 4 months ago as noted above.



Please give us a call to discuss and so we can further make our point.



Thanks.



---Steve



425-753-8897

swinfield@hotmail.com<mailto:swinfield@hotmail.com>



-------------------------------------------------------



Steve Winfield - Credit Score 470ish (was 690ish in February of this year)


·       Employer
o   Trustifi - Starting July 22
·       Channel Manager/Technical Sales
·       Income: $110k base plus $80k targeted commission
o   TrustArc - Ending July 21
·       Channel Manager/Technical Sales
·       Income: $100k base plus $42k actual commission
·       Rental History
o   Essex Properties (10/17-Current) - $3450/Month
·       Bothell, WA and transferred lease to Pleasanton, CA
·       (650) 655-7800
·       Galloway@essex.com<mailto:Galloway@essex.com>
o   AMC Apartments (2/15-10/17) - $2400/Month
·       Sammamish, WA
·       (801) 565-7430
·       KnollsAtEnglewoodHill@AMCllc.com<mailto:KnollsAtEnglewoodHill@AMCllc.com>



Gaby Sanchez Rojas (Winfield) - Credit Score 680ish


·       Employer
o   All in One Dental
·       Dental Assistant Manager
·       Income: $36k
·       Rental History
o   Civic Square (03/09-Current) - $2800/Month
·       Pleasanton, CA
·       (855) 207-7419
·       Info@liveatcivicsquare.com<mailto:Info@liveatcivicsquare.com>

[Gaby and Steve. Note the "Coming Soon" concept.  :-)]

[Our kids.]
The two in the front are both off at college.
The two in the back still live with us.

body:
Hi Kate…

I hope you are well and enjoying your last free time before the Navy…😊

I would like to bounce an idea off you and get your honest opinion.

I have an idea of making a business out of enabling online influencers, AI avatars and call agents to engage with their customers and communities at scale and at a very attractive price point.

On the back end it would be tying various AI models together: LMMs for online chatting, image creation models (like Flux or Stable Diffusion), Video Models (i.e. Runway or Kling) and voice engines like 11 Labs.

This would allow the system to reactively engage with the client like everyone else. However, through a series of triggering mechanisms and a proprietary context method (ironically called “Intelligent Forgetting”) we can help people and companies have AI proactively reach out to customers and clients in a manner that makes the AI seem more human and caring. As a result, the customer is more likely to have greater satisfaction which leads to more upsells and stronger chance for renewal.

Revenue will come from a micro economy that can be paid for by a business, by a creator, or by an individual.

On its surface, do you think this sounds interesting at all? Is this something you could see the market wanting to know more about?

I am wanting to get some other people’s thought on it before I set up a Kickstarter campaign to raise funds.

Thanks for your time and opinion.

---Steve

425-753-8897
subject: Business idea

Hi. Here is a question for you. In the last few months, my credit score has dropped from low 700s to upper 400s because I had no choice but to stop paying  some bills so I could make sure I kept feeding my family.

My new job has a pay raise and let's me escape the ridiculously high cost of living in the Bay area.

If my wife's credit is in the upper 600s (with 2800/month icons) and I am in the upper 400s, (with $220k / annual) do we have chance at renting the house?

---Steve

As an extra, I have a number of people that could help provide this fractional knowledge as a side gig to their current roles, so it definitely can scale.

For you, that means monthly income continuing to grow as we get more companies interested and more fractional assignments in place.

________________________________
From: Steve Winfield <swinfield@hotmail.com>
Sent: Friday, January 23, 2026 6:29:28 AM
To: Travis Boak <boaktravis6@gmail.com>

Role Overview

Palo Alto Networks is seeking a senior global alliances executive to architect, scale, and lead its cloud and strategic partner ecosystem worldwide. This role owns the design and execution of partner programs across hyperscalers, technology partners, GSIs, MSSPs, and regional channel ecosystems.

The VP of Global Cloud Alliances will play a critical role in Palo Alto Networks’ cloud security growth strategy, bridging legacy channel distribution with modern cloud-native go-to-market motions while driving measurable revenue impact at global scale.

Key Responsibilities

  *   Define and execute global cloud alliances and strategic partner strategy

  *   Own relationships with hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and key technology partners

  *   Architect partner program structures, incentives, and enablement frameworks

  *   Drive joint GTM motions with GSIs, MSSPs, and cloud ecosystem partners

  *   Partner with Sales, Product, and Marketing to align partner strategy with revenue goals

  *   Build and scale global partner operations, portals, and enablement platforms

  *   Track and optimize partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue

  *   Recruit, lead, and develop senior global alliances leaders

Ideal Candidate Profile

  *   15+ years of experience in global alliances, channels, or partner leadership within cybersecurity or enterprise software

  *   Deep expertise in cloud security, cyber risk, and enterprise channel ecosystems

  *   Proven experience building and scaling global partner programs at enterprise scale

  *   Strong understanding of both legacy distribution and cloud-native GTM models

  *   Executive presence with ability to influence C-suite partners and internal leadership

Compensation (Executive Level)

Base Salary:
$270,000 – $350,000

Annual Bonus:
40% – 60% of base

Equity (RSUs):
$400,000 – $900,000+ (multi-year vesting)

Total Annual Compensation (All-In):
$700,000 – $1.4M+

Why This Role Is Critical

  *   Ownership of Palo Alto Networks’ most strategic global alliances

  *   Direct influence on cloud security revenue growth and partner-driven scale

  *   High visibility with executive leadership and hyperscaler partners

  *   Clear path to SVP-level leadership within a top-tier cybersecurity platform

________________________________
From: The Gottman Institute <connect@gottman.com>
Sent: Thursday, October 11, 2018 5:00:11 PM
To: Elizabeth Elizabeth
Subject: Know someone who's single?

If you're looking to get back into the dating game, or you know someone who is, our new Seven Principles for Singles program is a great place to start.
[The Seven Principles for Singles]<https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-r/>

January 12, 2019 // Seattle, Washington



If you're looking to get back into the dating game, or you know someone who is, our new Seven Principles for Singles<https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-y/> program is a great place to start.

This is not a singles mixer. While you'll meet and interact with other singles, our goal is to give you the tools you'll need to have a successful relationship.

This one-day workshop is based on John Gottman’s New York Times bestseller The Seven Principles for Making Marriage Work. Although originally written for married couples, the Seven Principles apply to all relationships, including between partners, family, friends, and co-workers.

Here's what real singles have said about the workshop.

"I am convinced that choosing who I will start a family with is the most important decision I will ever make, and this workshop has given me the tools to make a better decision and then work on maintaining a successful marriage.”

“Thank you for creating this. I’ve been looking for something to guide me toward creating a healthy relationship for a while.”


LEARN MORE<https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-j/>

[Facebook]<https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-t/>    [Twitter] <https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-i/>    [Instagram] <https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-d/>  [Pinterest] <https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-l-pcbht-yhoyktkki-h/>
Our mailing address is:
2101 4th Ave Suite 1750, Seattle, WA 98121
Preferences<https://thegottmaninstitute.updatemyprofile.com/j-pcbht-0299C2F1-yhoyktkki-k>  |  Unsubscribe<https://thegottmaninstitute.cmail19.com/t/j-u-pcbht-yhoyktkki-u/>

Happy to jump on the phone and chat if you’d like; I certainly have some free time on my hands now. 😊

Thanks.

---Steve

On Thu, Oct 8, 2020 at 7:31 PM Vaibhav Mehrotra <vmehrotra@secuvy.com<mailto:vmehrotra@secuvy.com>> wrote:
Hi Steve,

Sharing the details of the contract. Please review, once you confirm I will put it in the contract and send you for signing. If this is good you can start reaching to your network, I will share required documentation about the company and we can meet early next week.



1. Duties and Responsibilities


1. Reach out to Customers/Partners for selling Secuvy - Data Privacy Platform.

2. For each deal you will help Secuvy from introduction to closing the deal in the sales process. Secuvy management team will be working together at every step.



2. Compensation


A) For each customer deal, a commission of 20% on Annual Contract Value (ACV) will be paid once the Secuvy closes the deal with the customer and the customer pays the ACV fees to Secuvy. Payment of commission to Sales Consultant will be done within 3 weeks from the day Secuvy receives payment from the customer.



B) If the Sales consultant closes the deal with a customer for a multi-year contract of 3yrs, a commission of 25% will be paid on the total amount. Commission will be paid once Secuvy closes the deal with the customer and the customer pays the full contract fees to Secuvy. Payment of commission to Sales Consultant will be done within 3 weeks from the day Secuvy receives payment from the customer.


2. In the case of ISV and other indirect/channel sales, 20% commission on ACV on the portion of the revenue received by Secuvy. Payment of commission to Sales Consultant will be done within 3 weeks from the day Secuvy receives payment from the customer.



3. Contract is at will and can be cancelled anytime with immediate effect.

Best,


--
